prompt: A low-profile shuttle axis. A broad base plate supports a shallow rail set and a boxy moving shuttle with visible bearing length and stop hardware at both ends. The shuttle is one prismatic joint along the rail centerline.

A dual-pedestal spindle module. A heavy foot supports two pedestals that carry one thick shaft, with the shaft projecting past one support to a plain faceplate. One continuous revolute joint spins the shaft about the axis set by the pedestals.

An industrial twenty-four-bin parts sorter cabinet with a narrow-tall rectangular steel body and twenty-four removable drawers in six rows of four. Each drawer pulls out prismatically on steel slides. The cabinet body mounts on two fixed floor feet.

A tower-mounted roll-axis spindle module. A short pedestal rises from a heavy foot and ends in a compact axis package whose moving member is visually smaller than the support. One revolute joint spins the output flange about its own longitudinal shaft axis between fixed supports.

A service fixture built as an XY stage. A fixed base rail set supports a long moving carriage, and that carriage carries a compact orthogonal slide with a top pad. Two orthogonal prismatic joints provide the X and Y motions.

A twin-rail forklift mast module. A fixed upright pair supports a compact moving carriage that wraps the guides and carries two forks at its lower edge. The carriage moves on one vertical prismatic joint.

An under-slung shutter blade assembly. A top support carries several hanging blades on separate pivots, giving the array a different support layout while keeping the same hard-surface blade geometry. Each blade rotates on its own revolute joint.

A screw-in light bulb seated in a socket. A bulb envelope sits above a threaded metal base, and the socket body holds the mating threaded collar. A record turntable. A plinth supports a central platter and a tonearm base off to one side. Joints: the platter rotates continuously about vertical, and the tonearm swings on a revolute pivot at the tonearm base. Use one central support under the rotating stage.

A tower-mounted coaxial rotary fixture. A grounded foot and short center post carry multiple coaxial carriers stacked one above the other. Each carrier is a revolute joint about the common centerline.
A side-wall three-stage extension unit. A grounded side plate supports an outer channel, a middle runner, and an inner runner. The middle and inner runners slide through serial prismatic joints.

A compact service carriage with elbow linkage. A grounded guide supports one moving carriage that carries a shoulder link and a shorter outer link. The carriage is prismatic, then shoulder and elbow provide two revolute joints.

A low-profile dual-rail gantry assembly. A broad base plate carries two guideways, a shallow moving beam, and a compact cross-slide carriage. One prismatic joint moves the beam across the base, and a second prismatic joint moves the cross-slide across the beam.

A pair of thick-frame wayfarer glasses with broad straight temples. The rigid front frame has visible barrel hinges at the outer corners and no separate nose-pad hardware. The two temples fold inward on separate revolute hinge axes at the frame ends.

A compact service slide-link-slide module. A broad grounded body supports one carriage, one pivot frame, and one short terminal slider with plain hard edges. The carriage is prismatic, the pivot frame is revolute, and the terminal slider is prismatic.
A compact service joystick module. A squat grounded housing supports a cardan pair and a short lever with a simple root collar. The cardan pair provides two orthogonal revolute joints.

An automotive windshield sun visor with a padded visor panel, a roof-mounted pivot bracket, and a side retaining clip near the headliner, plus a vanity-mirror cover on the panel face. the visor panel remains clearly separate from the hinge rod and clip hardware, and the cover sits as a distinct inset flap in the visor panel. The visor panel rotates downward on its roof-mounted hinge rod and swivels sideways on the secondary pivot at the bracket, and the vanity-mirror cover rotates on a small hinge in the panel face. Automotive interior scale.

A searchlight tower with a pan-tilt lamp head. A mast support carries a yoke, and the lamp body sits between the yoke arms. Joints: the head pans on a vertical revolute base and tilts on a horizontal revolute yoke axis. Use paired side supports framing the rotating stage.

A dual-seat swing set with two independent hanging seats under one beam. The long top beam supports two separate seat assemblies on their own side hanger links. Each seat assembly rotates independently on its own pair of hanger pivots beneath the top beam.

A large turbofan engine with a cylindrical nacelle, a front fan assembly, and a variable exhaust nozzle at the rear. The nacelle keeps the fan, core, and nozzle clearly aligned on one centerline. The fan rotates continuously about the main longitudinal axis and the nozzle petals rotate on short pivots around the rear exhaust ring. Aircraft-engine display scale.

A low-profile hugger ceiling fan with a round motor housing mounted directly to the ceiling plate without a downrod. Five blade irons with blades attach to the motor hub. The blade assembly rotates continuously about the vertical axis.

A teaching-lab monocular microscope with a wide foot, a straight arm, a monocular tube, and a square stage on a cross-slide block. The full stage block translates prismatically fore and aft on its support, the objective turret rotates on the optical-axis revolute joint, and a stage-control knob rotates on a small horizontal shaft at the slide block side.

A wide-mouth soup blender with a tall wide cylindrical jug on a round base. The jug locks on the base with a revolute twist mount. A hinged pour spout lid on the jug opens on a revolute hinge. The blade rotates continuously about the vertical axis.

A narrow overshot waterwheel with a suspended inlet box and a sliding regulator plate. The wheel is mounted on a central shaft between two side bearings, and the inlet box above the rim has a regulator plate moving in short guides. The wheel rotates continuously on the horizontal shaft, and the regulator plate slides prismatically in the inlet box guides.

A hand syringe. A cylindrical barrel holds a plunger rod and plunger head, ending in a nozzle tip. Joints: the plunger translates prismatically along the barrel axis. Use one dominant fixed guide and a smaller moving carriage.

A horizontal-axis wind turbine with a tapered tower, a nacelle, and a three-blade rotor hub at the front. The nacelle stays clearly mounted on a yaw bearing at the tower top instead of reading as part of the tower shaft. The rotor spins continuously about the main shaft axis and the nacelle yaws about the vertical tower axis. Utility wind-turbine scale.

A compact executive globe with a low cylindrical base and a tilted support fork. The sphere is held between polar pivots inside a short forked meridian, and the whole support stands on a swiveling base collar. The globe rotates continuously about its polar axis, and the support fork rotates about a vertical axis at the base collar.

A twin-column candy vending machine with two clear canisters over a shared cabinet, two selector knobs, and a central collection chute. Each selector knob rotates continuously on its own horizontal shaft, and the collection flap rotates on a small lower hinge at the chute mouth.

A portable compact flatbed scanner with a slim rectangular body and a lid that hinges from the long rear edge. The lid opens on two revolute hinges at the lid's long rear edge. An internal CCD scan head moves prismatically along the scan axis.

A compact city kick scooter with a folding handlebar stem, a folding rear fender brake, and a folding deck hinge. The handlebar stem folds at the upper neck clamp on a revolute hinge. The deck folds at the center hinge on a revolute joint for compactness. Both wheels rotate continuously.

A tabletop serving lazy Susan with an outer ring and an inner center platter on independent bearings. The larger lower disc supports the outer serving ring, while a smaller round center platter is mounted above on its own bearing. The outer ring rotates on one vertical revolute axis, and the center platter rotates independently on a second vertical axis.

A long-reach service TV wall mount without the panel. A grounded wall bracket carries two arm links and a compact output frame, with the links distinctly longer than the head support. The two links fold on revolute joints, and the head adds one swivel and one tilt revolute joint.

category_title: Laptop stand with articulated height adjustment
prompt: A laptop stand with a wide base slab, two vertical rear guide rods, and one rigid tray carried by a single carriage spanning both rods. The tray remains a single rigid supported body and the twin rods act as matched counterparts guiding the same carriage. The carriage slides prismatically along the vertical guide direction, and the tray rotates on a horizontal tilt hinge above the carriage.

A cinema prime lens with a large-diameter barrel in a matte-black housing carrying separate engraved focus and iris ring bands, a wide front element housing, and a rear mount ring with a locking lever. The focus ring is a knurled rubber-gripped band rotating on the outer barrel, and the iris ring is a narrower smooth band behind it. A lens shade bayonets onto a front flange ring.

A USB drive with a swivel cover. A compact body carries the connector, and a U-shaped cover wraps around the body on a side pin. Joints: the cover rotates continuously about the side pivot axis. Use a cleaner silhouette with fewer secondary breaks around the rotating part.

A stick vacuum with a folding wand. A motor body connects to a straight wand and a floor head, with the fold joint placed between the body and the wand. Joints: the fold joint is revolute about a horizontal axis, and the floor head pitches on a second revolute hinge. Nest the joints tightly so the whole assembly reads as dense and compact.

A wide-field bino-viewer refractor pair on a parallelogram arm mounting. Two slim cylindrical refractor tubes are fixed to a shared bridge bar. The bridge bar connects to a parallelogram arm assembly through two sequential revolute joints that maintain orientation. The parallelogram arm base rotates on a continuous azimuth bearing.

A chimney-style kitchen range hood with a lower intake canopy and a tall upper chimney cover. A slim front control panel carries several push-buttons arranged in a row across the front face. Each button is a short prismatic plunger that moves normal to the panel with brief travel into the housing.

A sensitive bench drill press with a slim column, a small tilting table, and a sensitive quill feed head with a fine-touch lever. The table slides prismatically up the column. The table tilts on a revolute hinge at the table mount bracket.

A travel camcorder with a slim main body, a prominent front lens barrel, and a rectangular flip screen with rounded corners. The flip screen rotates on a vertical side hinge at the body flank, the lens focus ring rotates continuously around the barrel, and a small media-card hatch rotates on a bottom-edge hinge at the side of the handgrip. Small electronics-product scale.

A simple rice cooker with a cylindrical body, a hinged upper lid, and a rotary cook-warm control dial on the front face. The lid rotates on a horizontal hinge at the rear rim, and the control dial rotates continuously on its central axle. Small household appliance scale.

category_title: Pedal trash bin with hinged lid
prompt: A hospital waste bin with a tall rectangular body, a flap-style top lid, and a wide textured foot pedal, plus a soft-close damper cover on the hinge side. the foot pedal remains a distinct lever under the front wall, and the damper cover sits as a separate cap over the rear hinge mechanism. The lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge and the wide foot pedal rotates on a lower front pivot, and the damper cover rotates on a short hinge at the back. Clinical waste-bin scale.

A compact tool wrist with a yaw collar at the root, a pitch yoke in the middle, and a short roll spindle carrying a flange at the nose. The three axis supports should be easy to distinguish by shape and scale. Yaw is a revolute joint about a vertical axis, pitch is a revolute joint about a horizontal cross-axis, and roll is a revolute joint about the tool axis.

A lightweight XC mountain bike suspension fork with a round steerer, two telescoping lower stanchions, and a wide flat handlebar on a stem. Each lower stanchion slides prismatically up into an upper crown leg. The stem clamps on a revolute joint. The handlebar rotates in the stem clamp on a revolute joint.

A folding A-frame step ladder. A front climbing frame and a rear support frame meet at a top cap, with wide treads on the front frame. Joints: the rear frame folds relative to the front frame on a single revolute hinge at the top cap. Keep the open frame nearly symmetric about the center plane.

A robotic leg module for a walking machine. An upper leg housing carries a thigh link, shank link, and compact ankle-foot section. Joints: hip revolute about a lateral axis, knee revolute about a lateral axis, ankle revolute about a lateral axis. Keep the wrist or end section very compact relative to the upstream links.

A compact bar-mounted casino machine with a shallow cabinet, a single screen, a short button shelf, and a coin tray underneath. A selector knob at the side of the shelf rotates continuously about its shaft, the coin tray flap rotates on a horizontal hinge under the front lip, and a service panel at the cabinet rear rotates on a vertical side hinge.

A wall-mounted medical-grade air purifier with a flat-back rectangular housing, a front-facing filter access panel hinged at the base on a continuous knuckle hinge, and a narrow vented face grille. The filter door folds downward on the base hinge to expose the filter cavity, and visible detent latches at the upper corners hold it shut. The result should feel solid and well-proportioned, with all motion tied to real mechanical supports.

A broad utility knife with a side-mounted blade lock lever. The straight handle surrounds the sliding blade track and carries a short lever near the center. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the blade lock lever rotates on a small side pivot.

A portable synth case with a hard lower shell and a broad hinged lid. The rectangular lower base supports the instrument bed, and the lid spans the full opening on a rear hinge with a pair of front catches. The lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge axis, and the two catches rotate on their own support pivots at the front edge.

A folding-bike compact crankset with shortened crank arms on a sealed bottom bracket, a single small-diameter chainring, and folding pedals whose outer halves hinge inward on a knuckle joint at the crank arm boss. Each pedal outer body folds flat against the crank arm on a spring-tensioned hinge for compact storage.

A low-profile portal module. A broad fixed base supports two side plates and a beam, with a short bridge carriage and a compact vertical stage hanging from it. The bridge carriage is a prismatic joint on the beam and the hanging stage is a vertical prismatic joint on the bridge carriage.

A pet door with a rigid flap and a hinged interior trim cover. The outer frame is fixed, the flap is mounted at the top edge, and an inner trim cover swings open on a side hinge for cleaning access. The flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and the trim cover rotates on its side hinge at the inner frame.

A knee scooter with a split rear axle and a tilting knee support. The rigid frame carries two front steering wheels, two rear support wheels, and a knee platform on a short side-supported tilt bracket. The front steering assembly yaws on a vertical axis, the four wheels spin on their axles, and the knee platform rotates on a transverse pivot at its support bracket.

A wall service hatch with a simple upward-opening panel. The shallow perimeter frame is fixed, and the panel is hinged along the top edge with a small rotary latch near the bottom center. The panel rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and the latch rotates on its short local shaft.

A freestanding portable yard ramp with an inclined steel drive surface, two continuous side rails with cross-brace rungs, a horizontal platform at the top, and two telescoping leg posts with locking pins and swivel caster feet supporting the top end. The bottom terminates in a lip hook plate that engages a dock edge.

A split presenter clicker with a rectangular lower base and a detachable clicker top section. The clicker top slides off on a short prismatic joint along the body's long axis. A small button guard on the clicker top pivots on a revolute hinge.

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A two-branch rotary fixture. A central mast rises from a grounded base and carries two radial hub blocks at staggered heights, each with a short arm ending in a pad or fork. Each arm is its own revolute joint about a local supported hub axis, and the two branches should remain mechanically independent.

A side-plate lever chain. A fixed cheek supports a sequence of flat-sided links with compact pin bosses, ending in a small end tab. The links are joined by three revolute joints on parallel supported axes.

A revolute-revolute-prismatic chain. A base bracket carries a shoulder link, an elbow link, and a short sliding tip stage. The first two joints are serial revolutes bending in one plane at about 120 degrees each way and 90 degrees each way, and the tip stage slides about 60 mm along its own axis.

A stockroom hand truck with a rounded frame and a loop handle. The frame carries a fixed toe plate, two large rear wheels, and a small kickstand leg under the back edge. The rear wheels spin on their axle, and the kickstand leg rotates on a small hinge beneath the frame.

A combined washer-dryer unit with a wide rectangular body, a front porthole door, and a pull-out soap dispenser drawer. The porthole door opens on revolute hinges at the door's vertical edge. The soap dispenser drawer slides prismatically out from the top control panel housing.

A boxy food processor with a straight-sided base, a locking bowl, a tall feed pusher, and a front keypad with five rectangular buttons beside one selector dial. The bowl twists on a short continuous coupling at the base, the pusher slides prismatically in the feed tube, and the slicing disc rotates continuously on the vertical center shaft. The selector dial rotates continuously, and all five buttons are separate small prismatic push controls. The bowl and feed tube should remain clearly hollow with open internal volume for ingredients. Countertop appliance scale.

A passenger blimp with a streamlined main envelope, a long under-slung cabin, a cruciform tail, and one propeller pod on each side of the hull near the cabin. The propellers spin continuously on their local shaft axes, and the vertical and horizontal tail control surfaces move on simple revolute hinges at the tail trailing edges. Large lighter-than-air vehicle scale with a clean, recognizable form.

A stackable rolling toolbox with a stout lower case, a telescoping handle frame, and a lift-up top lid carrying a molded grip. The telescoping handle frame slides prismatically upward behind the body, the top lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge, a shallow side accessory drawer slides prismatically from one flank, and the transport wheels rotate continuously. Utility storage scale.

A desk fan with a tilting head. A base supports a guarded fan head between two side brackets. Joints: the fan rotor spins continuously about its axle, and the head tilts on a horizontal revolute axis through the side brackets. Use paired side supports framing the rotating stage.

A roof-mounted remote station with a rotating turret, a tilting weapon cradle, and a top service lid. The turret shell stands on a yaw ring over the roof, and the service lid spans the top of the weapon housing. The turret rotates on a vertical axis, the weapon cradle rotates on a horizontal pitch axis, and the service lid rotates on a top hinge along one edge.

A glossy enamel stand mixer with a teardrop-shaped motor head, a bright steel bowl on a shallow carriage, and a balloon whisk beneath the nose. Articulation should stay canonical: the bowl moves on one short prismatic slide from the base, the head tilts on one rear revolute hinge, the whisk spins continuously on a vertical axis, a base-mounted speed dial rotates on a small revolute joint, and a base-mounted lock button translates on a short prismatic joint.

A heavy-duty pickup tailgate with a fold-out grab handle. The rectangular gate panel hangs from the lower bed edge, and a short grab handle is recessed into the upper inner face. The tailgate rotates on the lower horizontal hinge axis, and the grab handle rotates on two small side pivots in its recess.

An artist's single-post adjustable easel with a round base, a telescoping center post, and a tilt cradle at the top. The center post extends prismatically from the base tube. The canvas tilt cradle tilts on a revolute hinge at the post top.

A ring-and-cradle pitch-roll head. A grounded base bracket carries an outer roll frame, and the roll frame carries an inner pitch cradle with a small output face. The outer frame rolls on a revolute joint about its main axis, and the inner cradle pitches on a perpendicular revolute joint.

An undershot waterwheel. Two side frames support a horizontal axle carrying a paddle wheel below a simple channel or trough edge. Joints: the wheel rotates continuously about the axle axis. Use paired side supports framing the rotating stage.

A naval missile launcher with a rotating deck pedestal and a twin-rail elevation frame. The base is fixed to the deck, a yaw ring carries the elevation frame, and two launch rails are fixed to the frame arms. The pedestal rotates on a vertical revolute axis, and the rail frame pitches on a horizontal revolute axis at the side trunnions.

A countertop centrifugal juicer with an offset feed chute, a clear upper lid, and a slim pusher matched to the chute opening. A small flap cover is hinged over the top of the chute when the pusher is removed, and a side lever controls the operating mode. The pusher slides prismatically along the chute axis, the main lid rotates on a rear hinge, the chute flap rotates on a small top hinge, the side lever rotates on a short pivot, and the juicing basket rotates continuously about the vertical motor axis.

A portable pyramid metronome with two folding stabilizer legs at the base corners. A tapered housing on a rectangular base carries a pendulum rod with a sliding cylindrical weight above; two leg extensions fold outward from the base corners on revolute joints for stability on angled surfaces, folding flush against the base for transport. Pendulum swings on a revolute at the housing-top pivot; weight slides prismatically along the rod; each stabilizer leg pivots on a revolute at its base-corner hinge; winding key on the rear face rotates continuously.

A premium home router with four antennas and a rotary brightness knob. The faceted body carries two antennas at the rear and one on each side, with a small knob near the front edge for status lighting. The four antennas rotate on their base hinges, and the brightness knob rotates on its short local shaft.

A traditional windmill with a rotating sail hub. A tower body supports a cap and a front hub carrying multiple lattice blades. Joints: the blade hub rotates continuously about the windmill shaft axis, and the cap rotates about the vertical tower axis. Make the rotating member small relative to a broader fixed support.

A desk-mounted monitor arm shown without a screen. A clamp base carries a lower arm, an upper arm, and a compact VESA head with a tilt bracket. The shoulder, elbow, and head swivel are revolute joints about vertical axes, and the head tilt is a revolute joint about a horizontal axis at the mounting plate.

A compact karaoke speaker with a telescoping trolley handle, rear transport wheels, and a top deck carrying a large knob and two mode buttons, plus a power rocker and two menu buttons. the telescoping rails are clearly visible behind the upper shell, and the rocker stays distinct from the two smaller buttons beside it. The trolley handle slides prismatically on its twin rails, the rear wheels spin continuously on their axle, and the main knob rotates continuously, and the power rocker rotates on a short pivot and the two menu buttons each depress independently. Portable karaoke-speaker scale.

A dual-head floor pump with a tall barrel, a T-handle, a low gauge, and a clamping valve head at the hose end, plus a folding handle grip around the top bar. the hose head stays visibly supported by the end of the flexible hose rather than floating loose, and the grip remains a supported sleeve on the crossbar. The T-handle and rod translate prismatically on the pump axis and the clamp lever at the valve head rotates on its short hinge pin, and the grip rotates continuously about the crossbar axis. Bike-workshop pump scale.

category_title: Dishwasher with dropdown door and sliding racks
prompt: A contemporary built-in dishwasher with a concealed top strip, two sliding racks, and twin fold-down mug shelves integrated into the upper rack. The dropdown door rotates on a lower horizontal hinge, both racks slide prismatically on their side rails, and the two spray arms rotate continuously on central hubs in the tub. The mug shelves rotate on matched side pivots, the start button and three option buttons on the top strip are separate small prismatic push buttons, and the rinse-aid cap on the door liner rotates continuously. The tub and door interior should remain thin-walled and hollow. Standard kitchen scale.

A three-joint robotic arm for pick-and-place work. A pedestal base carries a shoulder housing, upper arm link, forearm link, and compact wrist head. Joints: shoulder revolute about vertical, elbow revolute about horizontal, wrist revolute about the forearm axis. Nest the joints tightly so the whole assembly reads as dense and compact.

A stationary exercise bike. A floor frame supports the flywheel housing, crank set, seat post, and handlebar mast. Joints: the crank rotates continuously about the bottom-bracket axis, the flywheel rotates continuously about its axle, and the seat post adjusts prismatically along its column. Make the left and right supports nearly symmetric about the centerline.

An underwater ROV drone with a rectangular frame body, four thruster propellers in cross-mount positions, and a tilting camera pod at the nose. Each thruster propeller rotates continuously about its own axis. The camera pod tilts on a revolute joint at the nose bracket.

A drill-press cross-slide vise with a base casting, an X-direction sliding stage riding on a dovetail rail cast into the base, and a Y-direction jaw on top with its own flat rail, each stage driven by a small threaded handwheel at one end. The jaw faces carry flat serrated gripping surfaces, and the dovetail geometry is visible on the base casting edge. Keep the base, X stage, Y jaw, and two handwheels as separate rigid parts with the rail geometry clear to view.

A compact travel scooter with a folding handlebar and a slim deck. The narrow deck carries the front steering column, and the handlebar crossbar folds down at the top of the stem. The front fork yaws on a vertical steering axis, the wheels spin continuously on their axles, and the handlebar crossbar rotates on a hinge at the top of the stem.

category_title: Top-load washing machine with hinged lid
prompt: A premium top-load washer with a soft-close glass lid, a detergent drawer in the top deck, a bleach-fill flap near the rear edge, and a console with one central dial plus five program buttons. The lid rotates upward on a rear hinge, the detergent drawer slides prismatically from the deck, the bleach flap rotates on a small hinge, and the wash tub rotates continuously on the vertical axis. The dial rotates continuously, and the five buttons are separate prismatic push controls. The wash opening should read as a real hollow laundry chamber rather than a solid cap over the body. Premium domestic scale.

category_title: Desktop pencil sharpener with hand crank and drawer
prompt: A compact office pencil sharpener with a rounded shell, a rear hand crank, a lower front tray, and a sliding sharpening-mode selector beside the entry hole. The crank rotates continuously at the back corner, the tray slides prismatically from the lower front of the housing, and the mode selector slides prismatically along a short guide next to the pencil opening. Small desktop scale.

category_title: Adjustable weight bench with hinged backrest
prompt: A folding adjustable weight bench with a low tubular frame, a split seat and backrest, a rear support link, and front transport wheels tucked under the front crossmember. The backrest pivots on a transverse hinge relative to the center frame, the seat pivots on its own hinge ahead of the backrest, the rear support link rotates on a lower frame pivot, and the front transport wheels spin continuously on their axle. Compact apartment-gym scale.

A folding monitoring headphone with a split headband and telescoping earcup arms. The inner headband carries two sliding support arms that connect to folding earcup yokes. The earcups rotate on their fold hinges at the yokes, and the support arms slide prismatically within the headband frame.

A utility vehicle glove compartment with a left-hinged narrow door and a keyed cam latch. The fixed dash bin sits behind a rectangular opening, and the door is mounted to one side wall on exposed hinges. The door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, the key cylinder rotates on its local axis, and the internal cam rotates with the lock near the latching edge.

A long gaming graphics card with two large front axial fans and a smaller tail fan over a flow-through opening. The PCB and heatsink stack are wrapped by an angular shroud, with the tail opening cut through the back end of the card. All three fan rotors spin continuously about their local axes within the fixed shroud. Keep the fan rotors clipped into their circular frames within the shroud so the rotating fans stay seated on the card.

A compact stackable ventless condensation dryer with a slim rectangular body, a front door, and a removable lint filter panel. The front door opens on revolute hinges at the door's vertical edge. The lint filter slides prismatically out from the door opening rim. The drum rotates continuously inside.

A salon ring light with a side-mounted accessory arm. The ring head is held in a tilt yoke above a telescoping stand, and a short accessory arm folds from one side of the stand collar. The ring head rotates on its horizontal tilt axis, the stand column slides prismatically, and the accessory arm rotates on a small side hinge.

A toy roadster with an open cabin, a long front hood, a short rear deck, and four narrow wheels. The four wheels rotate continuously on their axles, the front hood rotates on a rear horizontal hinge, and the trunk lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge at the tail deck.

A square clamshell waffle maker with thick upper and lower cooking plates, a short front handle, and a rear hinge block. The top plate lifts on a single horizontal revolute hinge at the back, and a small temperature dial rotates on the side of the lower housing. Countertop scale with a broad, low body.

A family microwave with a side door, a jog dial, and a push-open button. The rectangular housing carries a large glazed door at one side and a control column with one dial and one button on the other. The door rotates on a vertical hinge axis, the jog dial rotates on its own shaft, and the push-open button moves prismatically into the panel.

A wide-format flip communicator with a wide square lower body carrying a full QWERTY keypad and a wide square upper lid carrying a large display. The upper lid opens on a revolute hinge at the hinge post at the top of the lower body about a horizontal axis. A small external display on the outer lid face is fixed. A battery door on the lower body rear slides prismatically.

A small propeller model airplane. A fuselage carries fixed wings and tail, with a nose propeller and a simple landing gear or display peg under the belly. Joints: the propeller spins continuously about the nose axis. Make the rotating element broad and shallow.

A compact balcony grill with a rounded lid and a side-mounted temperature knob. The cook body sits on a narrow stand, and the lid is supported from the back edge with a single small control knob on the side panel. The lid rotates upward on a rear hinge axis, and the side control knob rotates on its own small shaft.

category_title: Palm with multiple uncoupled finger chains
prompt: A robotic palm with four independent finger chains and a side-mounted thumb chain. The rigid palm block supports four evenly spaced finger roots along the front edge and a separate thumb base on one side. Each finger root rotates on its own base revolute axis at the palm, and the thumb base rotates on a separate side-mounted revolute axis.

A print-shop guillotine with a broad lever arm and a rotary lock handle. The rigid base supports the main blade arm at one side and a round locking handle near the hinge block. The blade arm rotates on the side hinge axis, and the lock handle rotates on its own short shaft by the hinge block.

A workstation laptop with a hinged display and a pop-open service door under the base. The heavy lower body carries the keyboard deck, and a small access panel is mounted on a hinge under the underside near one edge. The lid rotates on the rear hinge axis, the service door rotates on its small hinge beneath the base, and the keys move as short vertical plungers.

category_title: Lounge chair with independent backrest and footrest
prompt: A zero-gravity patio lounge chair with a rigid support frame and a separate footrest panel. The main seat-back frame is carried between two side supports, and a second panel is hinged ahead of the seat frame for the lower legs. The seat-back frame rotates on a transverse recline axis at the side supports, and the footrest rotates independently on a second transverse hinge at the front.

A deli-counter grill press with a heavy lower plate, a tall rear hinge tower, and a broad insulated lid handle spanning the front. The lid rotates on a stout horizontal revolute joint at the hinge tower, a grease drawer slides prismatically from the base front, and a round heat-control knob rotates on the left side of the housing. Rugged countertop food-service scale.

An ironing board with a folding leg trestle. A board top mounts on a base bracket, with one U-shaped leg frame under the middle and a small fixed rest at one end. Joints: the main leg frame folds on a horizontal revolute axis under the board. Keep the open frame nearly symmetric about the center plane.

Build a precision professional Trigger spray bottle tuned for calibration and repeatable alignment. Use a bottle plus trigger head with visible trigger-to-pump linkage with datum-friendly surfaces, index marks, adjustment features, and controlled gap language. Visual style can be minimal, but kinematics must be explicit. Trigger stroke should transmit through a visible lever-and-pump path. Keep a fully connected mechanical stack with no free-floating parts. No floating parts.

An overhead travelling gantry crane with a rectangular steel portal frame on two rails and a bridge girder. The bridge girder travels prismatically along the portal rails. A crab trolley slides prismatically along the bridge girder. A hook block hangs from the crab on a fixed suspension.

A basic chop-style miter saw with a rotating table, a pivot arm, and a guarded circular blade. The fixed base carries the miter table and rear arm pivot, and the blade motor housing sits at the end of the arm behind a lower blade guard. The blade spins continuously about its arbor axis, the arm rotates downward on a horizontal pivot at the rear, the miter table rotates about a vertical center axis on the base, and the lower blade guard rotates on its hinge as the arm lowers. Keep the saw arm clipped to the rear pivot block so the blade head stays supported as it chops down.

A lotion pump bottle with a square-shouldered container and a lockable pump head. The bottle body is fixed, while the neck supports a telescoping plunger and a nozzle head with a twist-lock style cap geometry. The plunger translates prismatically along the bottle axis, and the nozzle head rotates on the stem axis at the top of the pump.

category_title: Digital multimeter with tilt stand and rotary selector
prompt: A compact electronics multimeter with a rounded case, a central function dial, a rear stand, and a front control cluster made of two round buttons and two square buttons. The dial rotates continuously on the front panel, the stand rotates outward on a lower rear hinge, and each visible control button moves independently along its local prismatic press axis. Small desktop instrument scale.

A single-hole kitchen gooseneck faucet with a tall arc spout, a side lever, and a pull-out spray wand. The side lever rotates on a revolute joint about the valve axis. The gooseneck spout rotates on a continuous revolute bearing about the mounting stud vertical axis. The spray wand extends prismatically from the spout end.

A compact bench slider. A grounded base extrusion or rail supports a short carriage block with side guides and a flat top face for mounting. The carriage is one prismatic joint on the fixed guide axis.

A stepped rotary shaft assembly. A grounded rail carries two bearing blocks and one straight shaft with a drive collar, a center hub, and a small flange at the end. The shaft is one continuous revolute joint about its supported centerline.

A low-wafer yaw module. A broad circular base supports a thin rotating upper deck with a short center bearing body kept visible below the deck. One revolute joint turns the upper deck about the vertical centerline.

category_title: Sewing box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A small sewing box with a plain top lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Bias the design toward one large uninterrupted panel.
A hardwood tabletop tool caddy with a rectangular base and three short drawers on the front face flanked by two fixed divider walls. Each drawer slides prismatically on wooden bottom guides.

A telescoping gooseneck desk lamp with a rectangular weighted base, a three-stage telescoping post, and a two-link arm with a shade. The post telescopes prismatically in three nested stages. Each arm link connects on a revolute elbow. The shade tilts on a revolute joint at the tip.

A single-sash sliding window. A rectangular outer frame contains one fixed frame segment and one sliding sash. Joints: the sash translates prismatically along horizontal guide channels. Expose more of the rails or channels so the travel path is easy to read.

A tower-mounted roll stage. A short pedestal rises from a heavy foot and ends in a compact roll head whose spinning member is smaller than the support below it. One revolute joint spins the carried flange about the shaft axis.

A heavy-duty hinge. Two rectangular leaves are joined by alternating knuckles around a central pin, with one leaf fixed to a mounting block. One revolute joint runs along the pin axis with about 0 to 180 degrees of travel.

A bar stool with a pedestal base, a ring footrest, and a curved wood seat plus a hinged rear luggage hook. The seat rotates continuously on the vertical pedestal axis, and the luggage hook rotates downward on a short hinge at the rear underside of the seat. Hospitality stool scale.
A twin-post platen lift. A heavy lower base supports two posts and a compact moving platen that wraps both posts as one rigid body. The platen moves by one vertical prismatic joint along the posts.

An orthogonal XY stage. A grounded base carries a lower X slide with a rectangular carriage, and that carriage supports a crosswise Y slide with a top table. The lower slide and the upper cross-slide are prismatic joints on orthogonal horizontal axes.

Design a realistic, highly detailed mast-mounted fork carriage as a standalone mechanical study assembly for the sdk_hybrid pipeline, with a vertical mast frame, nested channels, and a fork-style moving carriage. Keep it as a pure mechanical assembly with explicit joints, guides, bearings, brackets, and support structure. Avoid product styling, decorative housings, or recognizable household or device forms.

A compact shutter-vane module. A grounded perimeter frame carries several evenly spaced vanes with short support lugs at both ends. The vanes are independent revolute joints, each carried by its own pair of side supports.

A screw-in light bulb seated in a socket. A bulb envelope sits above a threaded metal base, and the socket body holds the mating threaded collar. Joints: the bulb rotates continuously about the shared socket axis. Keep the rotating element centered on the body midline.

A record turntable. A plinth supports a central platter and a tonearm base off to one side. Joints: the platter rotates continuously about vertical, and the tonearm swings on a revolute pivot at the tonearm base. Use paired side supports framing the rotating stage.

A coaxial rotary fixture stack with a broad lower turntable, a mid-height drum ring, and a compact top platen mounted concentrically above it. The lower stage should feel foundational, the middle stage should feel like a secondary indexing body, and the top stage should feel like a small tooling interface. Use realistic detailing such as seal grooves, bolt circles, split retainers, and layered housings that imply real bearings and service access. The lower, middle, and top stages are individual revolute joints about the same vertical centerline. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A service fixture built as a nested three-stage slide. A grounded outer guide supports successive inner runners with plain rectangular front faces and practical overlap. The middle and inner runners translate on serial prismatic joints.

A prismatic-revolute-revolute chain. A base rail carries a moving carriage, the carriage carries a shoulder link, and a short forelink hangs from an elbow hinge. The root is prismatic about 150 mm, then two serial revolute joints bend in one plane at roughly 90 degrees each way and 75 degrees each way.

An under-beam gantry shuttle. A grounded rail pair supports a bridge whose smaller carriage hangs beneath the beam instead of riding on top. The bridge and hanging carriage are separate prismatic joints on perpendicular guide directions.

A skateboard with standard trucks. A deck supports front and rear truck hangers, each holding one axle and two wheels. Joints: each wheel spins continuously about its axle, and each truck steers on a revolute kingpin axis. Make the front support narrow and the rear support broad.

A wall-backed XYZ positioning assembly. A rigid support carries a horizontal carriage system and a compact vertical carriage nested on the upper moving stage. The assembly stacks three prismatic joints so each axis remains visibly supported by the stage beneath it.

A wafer-handling style gripper. A dense center block supports two long, slender fork jaws on mirrored guides so the fingers stay thin while the support hardware remains substantial. The two jaws translate as independent prismatic joints toward and away from the center.

A city upright bike fork with a round steerer and two blade legs, and a wide swept-back handlebar on a tall stem. The tall stem clamps to the steerer on a revolute joint for angle. The swept-back bar rotates in the stem clamp on a revolute joint.
