Counter seating scale.

A compact twin-post lift module. A heavy foot supports two round posts and an upper tie bar, and a wide moving plate wraps both posts as a single carriage. One prismatic joint lifts the carriage on the grounded columns.

An under-plate XY stage. A top table is carried by a crosswise upper carriage, which is itself carried by a longer lower carriage on a grounded base. The two stacked carriages are prismatic joints on perpendicular horizontal axes.

A compact forklift mast section. Two upright channels and a top crosshead support a lift carriage with a load backrest and paired forks. One vertical prismatic joint moves the carriage on the mast rails while the forks remain rigid to the carriage.

A framed louver bank with independent vanes. A grounded rectangular frame supports a row of rigid slats with visible end pivots and credible slat thickness. Each slat is its own revolute joint about its long pivot axis.

A screw-in light bulb seated in a socket. A bulb envelope sits above a threaded metal base, and the socket body holds the mating threaded collar. Joints: the bulb rotates continuously about the shared socket axis. Make the rotating element broad and shallow.

A record turntable. A plinth supports a central platter and a tonearm base off to one side. Joints: the platter rotates continuously about vertical, and the tonearm swings on a revolute pivot at the tonearm base. Make the rotating member small relative to a broader fixed support.

An open-frame coaxial rotary assembly. A fixed base and exposed central shaft support concentric rotary members with simple collars and spacing that make the stacked kinematics easy to read. Each concentric member rotates by its own revolute joint about the shared centerline.
An under-slung three-stage runner. A top support carries an outer sleeve with a middle runner and a smaller inner runner hanging beneath it. The middle and inner runners are serial prismatic joints.

A low-profile carriage carrying an elbow arm. A broad fixed rail supports a moving block, and the block carries two hinged links sized so the root stage still reads as the larger positioning system. The rail stage is prismatic, and the two carried joints are revolute shoulder and elbow joints.

A ladder-frame gantry axis. A fixed base frame carries twin rails, a stiff moving beam, and a short tool carriage centered on the beam. The beam slides on one prismatic axis and the tool carriage slides on a second orthogonal prismatic axis.

A skateboard with standard trucks. A deck supports front and rear truck hangers, each holding one axle and two wheels. Joints: each wheel spins continuously about its axle, and each truck steers on a revolute kingpin axis. Push the wheels farther outboard for a wider stance.

An XYZ cartesian stage. A grounded base carries a long X slide, the X carriage carries a crosswise Y slide, and the Y carriage carries a compact Z slide with a top tool plate. The X, Y, and Z axes are three stacked prismatic joints on orthogonal guide directions.

A low-profile twin-slide gripper. A broad center spine supports two shallow side slides and two compact jaws that close into a small rectangular work area. Each slide is an independent prismatic joint along the jaw closing direction.

category_title: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector
prompt: A bench transfer axis with a rotary tool nose. A fixed guide rail supports a moving saddle, while the saddle carries a short cylindrical output stage with clear local support. One prismatic joint positions the saddle, and one revolute joint spins the tool nose on the saddle.
A side-wall double-carriage module. A grounded side plate supports one linear carriage and a second carried slider. The first and second stages are prismatic joints in sequence.

A beam road blocker with a flat rectangular steel beam arm that lifts vertically inside a fixed frame housing. The beam arm lifts on a prismatic joint along the vertical axis inside the housing guides.

A hinged service access panel on an equipment face. A rectangular frame surrounds the opening, and a panel door hangs from one side with a latch edge opposite. Joints: the door is a single revolute panel about a vertical side hinge axis. Make the hinge hardware short and tucked close to the body.

A three-joint articulated reading floor lamp with a round base and three sequential link sections ending in a shade. Each link connects to the next on a revolute elbow joint. The shade tilts on a revolute joint at the tip.

A single-axis pitch cradle. Two side plates on a base support a centered yoke carrying a small faceplate between the arms. The yoke rotates as one revolute joint about a horizontal trunnion axis through the side supports.

An open-frame offset-axis rotary assembly. A grounded base disk, a side support arm, and a small upper stage form a clear two-level rotary structure without enclosing the moving members. The two rotary stages are independent revolute joints with a visible lateral offset between their axes.

A wall-backed telescoping slide module. A rigid back support carries an outer guide channel and one nested moving section that projects from it. The moving section is a prismatic joint on the fixed outer guide.

A multi-position convertible ladder with two A-frame hinge sections joined at pivot knuckles. Each main pivot knuckle locks at multiple angles on a revolute hinge. An extension fly section slides prismatically in the rear stile guides.

A drawer-like shuttle with a side-mounted pivot block and a small inspection paddle at the end of a short link. The sliding base and the hinged link should have clearly separate support structures. The shuttle is a prismatic joint along its guide axis and the paddle link is a revolute joint on the shuttle.

An Alpine wooden bell cote with a narrow rectangular timber frame, a pointed roof, and a single brass bell hung on a pivot beam inside the frame. The pivot beam is fixed across the frame uprights. The bell hangs on a revolute hinge at the headstock. The bell swings about the horizontal headstock axis.

category_title: Roof vent tower with hinged flap
prompt: A rooftop vent tower with a weather flap. An upright vent housing ends in a framed outlet, with one flap attached along the top edge. Joints: the flap is a single revolute panel about a horizontal top-edge axis. Pull the hinge hardware outward so the axis is easy to read.

category_title: Watch winder box with hinged lid
prompt: A watch winder presentation box. A box body supports a top lid and one internal cradle mounted on a small spindle. Joints: the lid is revolute about a horizontal rear hinge axis, and the cradle rotates continuously about its spindle. Pull the hinge hardware outward so the axis is easy to read.

A parking-access bollard with a narrow cylindrical post, a rounded top dome, and a flush circular sleeve set into paving. The post moves prismatically up and down within the sleeve, a top latch ring rotates continuously around the dome centerline, and a small inspection hatch at ground level rotates on a vertical hinge. Light roadside control-hardware scale.

A slim residential tower fan with a tall rectangular housing, a circular pedestal base, and two rotary control dials on the top cap. The internal blower wheel rotates continuously about the vertical center axis, the fan body oscillates on a vertical revolute joint above the base, and both control dials rotate on separate shafts in the top panel. Floor appliance scale.

A sliding security gate. A fixed post and frame support one gate leaf captured by a top track or bottom guide. Joints: the gate leaf translates prismatically along the track axis. Keep the moving member tightly nested inside the fixed guide.

A compact service yaw-pitch head. A broad grounded body supports a compact rotary lower stage and a small trunnion cradle above it. The lower stage is a yaw revolute joint and the upper cradle is a pitch revolute joint.

A museum adjustable directional track spot with a straight ceiling rail and a single spot head. The spot head slides prismatically along the rail. The spot housing rotates continuously on a revolute bearing about the rail axis. The lamp inside tilts on a revolute joint at the head knuckle.

A pedestal indexer with a round turntable base, a rigid upper mast mounted on the turntable, and a twin-rail vertical carriage on the mast. The base and mast should read as one rotating lower assembly carrying a clearly separate lift stage. The pedestal rotates on a vertical revolute axis and the upper carriage moves on a vertical prismatic axis.

A platform wheelchair lift with a flat square platform and a single vertical column post. The platform slides prismatically along the vertical column. A folding knee-guard panel at the platform edge pivots on a revolute hinge at the platform front edge.

A manual wheelchair. A chair frame supports two large rear wheels, two front casters, a seat, a backrest, and footrests. Joints: each rear wheel rotates continuously about its axle, and each front caster swivels about a vertical stem and spins continuously about its wheel axle. Push the wheels farther outboard for a wider stance.
An under-slung three-stage slide. A top support carries three nested sliders hanging beneath it in a clear root-to-tip chain. The nested stages are three serial prismatic joints.

A three-arm turnstile gate. A central column supports a rotor hub with three radial arms inside a fixed frame. Joints: the arm hub rotates continuously about the vertical center axis. Make the rotating member small relative to a broader fixed support.
A library card catalog desk with a wide work surface and a twelve-drawer card index cabinet as the base. Each catalog drawer slides prismatically on individual wooden guide rails.

A low picket garden gate with a lower surface bolt for holding it open. The rectangular leaf hangs on side hinges between two posts and carries a ring latch near the upper rail and a slim drop bolt near the lower rail. The leaf rotates on a vertical hinge axis, the ring latch rotates on its own pivot, and the drop bolt slides vertically through its guide brackets.

A wheelbarrow. A tray sits between two handles, with one front wheel in a fork and two resting legs at the rear. Joints: the front wheel rotates continuously about its axle. Use slimmer tubular members and a lighter open-frame body.

A wall-mounted inspection arm with a grounded base plate, three rigid links, and a small square pad at the end. The link lengths should vary enough to give the arm a clear shoulder, elbow, and wrist silhouette. The three articulations are revolute joints about parallel hinge axes in one plane.

A control cabinet with a tall narrow carcass, a single hinged outer door, and an inner hinged equipment sub-door. The outer door hinges on revolute knuckles at its left edge. The inner sub-door hinges on its own revolute knuckles inside the outer frame.

A parking lot floodlight on a square concrete base with a tall octagonal pole and two tilting flood heads. The pole is fixed to the base. Two outreach brackets are fixed to the pole top. Each flood head tilts on a revolute hinge at the bracket tip.

A large dish with a rear instrument hatch and an azimuth-elevation support. The reflector is mounted to an elevation yoke above a pedestal base, and a small hatch sits on the rear instrument box behind the dish. The base rotates on a vertical azimuth axis, the dish rotates on a horizontal elevation axis, and the hatch rotates on a small side hinge.

category_title: Desktop monitor with tilt swivel stand
prompt: A curved gaming monitor with a wide display shell, a split rear yoke, and a tripod-style base, plus a power rocker and three menu buttons at the underside of the bezel. the yoke arms visibly carry the screen rather than hiding the support inside the shell, and the rocker and buttons stay distinct from one another. The display tilts on a horizontal revolute joint between the yoke arms and the whole stand swivels continuously about a vertical axis where the yoke meets the base, and the power rocker rotates on a short local pivot and the menu buttons depress independently. Gaming-desk scale.

A compact service arm with a sliding head. A fixed base support carries a single rotary arm, and the outer portion of that arm supports a short sliding carriage. The root support provides one revolute joint and the head carriage provides one prismatic joint.

A wheelie bin with a top lid. A bin body sits on a rear axle with two wheels and carries one full-width lid along the back edge. Joints: the lid is revolute about a horizontal rear hinge axis, and the rear wheels spin continuously about the axle. Favor thick side walls and a heavy frame around the moving panel.

A compact service elbow arm. A grounded root bracket carries a deep upper arm, a shallower forearm, and a small terminal face. The arm is a two-joint revolute chain with shoulder and elbow articulation.

A push-pull plunger assembly like a latching rod mechanism. A cylindrical guide body supports a sliding plunger rod with a rear knob and a front clevis tip. The plunger is a single prismatic joint along the rod axis within the guide body.

A telescoping inspection mast with a rotating top head. Nested mast tubes rise from a fixed base frame, and the top stage carries a compact pan head with a mounting plate. The mast stages are prismatic joints along the vertical mast axis, and the head is a revolute joint about the same vertical axis at the top support.

A toaster oven with a broad lower door, two front dials, and a sliding broil rack inside the cavity. The door rotates downward on a lower hinge, the two dials rotate continuously on their short shafts, and the broil rack slides prismatically on straight side runners within the cavity. Home kitchen scale.

A vertical tool slide with a hinged head. A rigid column supports a moving carriage, and the carriage carries a short wrist plate between side brackets. The carriage is a prismatic joint along the vertical guide axis, and the wrist plate is a revolute joint about a horizontal hinge axis at the carriage front.

A long serial linkage with five slim box links, a fixed root foot, and a small square pad at the far end. The links should alternate slightly in length so the chain has a distinct rhythm instead of reading as identical bars. The four articulations are revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane.

A wall-mounted transfer arm with a stout backplate, an angled shoulder bracket, a tapered first link, a deep elbow knuckle, and a square end plate. The arm should feel like a load-bearing reach mechanism with different link lengths and a visibly offset elbow package. The shoulder and elbow rotate about parallel horizontal pivots from supported knuckle blocks, and the end plate sits on its own wrist revolute axis.

A service-gripper finger chain. A rigid root mount carries three flat-sided finger segments with exposed pin bosses and a squared fingertip. The finger uses three serial revolute joints in one bending plane.

A vacuum cleaner with an articulated wand. A main body connects to a rigid wand chain ending in a floor nozzle. Joints: the wand bends on one or two revolute elbows, and the floor nozzle pitches on a horizontal revolute hinge. Use a lighter open-frame link layout with fewer covers around the joints.

category_title: CCTV mast with pan-tilt camera head
prompt: A parking structure overhead CCTV mount with a horizontal beam bracket, a fixed drop-rod, and a pan-tilt dome housing. The dome housing pans on a continuous revolute bearing and tilts on a revolute tilt joint.

category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A three-branch rotary fixture. A central mast carries three radial arms on separate hub blocks at staggered heights, with each arm ending in a small pad or fork. Each arm has its own revolute joint about a local hub axis with about 120 degrees of travel, and the branches should be spaced so their motion envelopes stay visually separate.

A floor-standing drafting table with a heavy pedestal base, a telescoping central column, and a rectangular drafting board with a pencil tray below the front edge. The board rotates on a horizontal trunnion axis at the top of the column, the column slides prismatically within the pedestal sleeve, and the pencil tray drawer translates prismatically on straight runners under the board.

An outdoor patio chest cooler with a wide rectangular insulated body on four short legs and a hinged lid. The lid opens on two revolute hinge knuckles at the lid's rear edge. A drain plug on the front lower face is a short prismatic press-fit.

An under-slung hinge-slider-wrist unit. A top support carries a pivoting frame below it, the frame carries a short slider, and the slider carries a small wrist tab. The frame is revolute, the slider is prismatic, and the wrist tab is revolute.

category_title: Tackle box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A tackle box with a plain hinged lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge, with internal tray details kept fixed inside the body. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Make the hinge hardware short and tucked close to the body.

category_title: Standing desk with synchronous telescoping legs and articulated controls
prompt: A compact home-office standing desk with a narrow top, twin telescoping legs, and a small side control pod under one corner, plus a shallow keyboard tray under the front edge. the top frame remains visibly separate from the lifting columns, and the tray is mounted on short side guides below the top frame. The two leg stages slide prismatically within their outer columns as a synchronized pair, and the keyboard tray slides prismatically on its guides. Small home-office desk scale.

A compact optical device on a tripod with folding legs, a sliding center mast, and a protective front cover on the device face. The legs rotate on crown hinges, the center mast slides prismatically, the head pans on a vertical joint, the device tilts on a horizontal hinge, and the protective front cover rotates upward on a small hinge. Portable optics scale.

A single-leaf drawbridge. A fixed shore frame carries one bridge leaf on heavy side bearings. Joints: the bridge leaf is a single revolute panel about a horizontal shore-side axis. Pull the hinge hardware outward so the axis is easy to read.

A studio chair with a round seat, a backrest hinge, and a fold-down footrest bar. The seat is mounted above a central column on a wheeled base, with a short bar hinged below the front edge. The chair rotates on a vertical axis, the caster wheels spin and swivel, the backrest rotates on a hinge at the seat rear, and the footrest bar rotates on a small front hinge.
A compact service YZ stage. A fixed back support carries a short lateral slide and a visibly smaller hanging Z carriage. The lateral and vertical stages are orthogonal prismatic joints.

A side-wall swing-and-slide mechanism. A grounded side plate supports a single arm link with a carried extension stage near the front. The root arm joint is revolute and the carried extension is prismatic.

A folding support arm like the linkage on a task light or small gate stay. A base bracket carries three flat bar links in series and ends in a small attachment pad. The chain has three revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane so the arm can collapse near the base and extend outward.

A pedestal rotary trunnion fixture. A grounded pedestal supports a rotary platter, and the platter supports a compact trunnion frame with a tilting faceplate. The platter rotates on a vertical revolute axis, and the faceplate tilts on a supported horizontal revolute axis.

A screw-cap bottle. A bottle body rises to a threaded neck, capped by a separate screw closure. Joints: the cap rotates continuously about the bottle neck axis. Make the rotating element broad and shallow.

A clicking slider fidget toy with a rectangular flat housing and a center thumb slider that slides back and forth. The thumb slider moves prismatically along the housing's long axis. A ratchet click mechanism engages at each end on a short revolute detent.

A pneumatic-lift overbed table with a rolling C-base, a rectangular tray, a gas-spring column cover, and a paddle release beneath the right side, plus locking casters at the base corners. the support stack stays visibly nested rather than collapsing into one solid post, and the wheel forks remain individually visible under the base frame. The lift column slides prismatically within the outer sleeve, the tray rotates on a tilt hinge at the column head, the release paddle rotates on a short pivot, and the four casters spin continuously on their axles, and the caster lock tabs rotate on their small local pivots. Clinical room scale.

A combination microwave-oven with a wide rectangular housing, a front door that swings sideways, and a rotating glass turntable inside. The microwave door swings on a revolute hinge at the door's vertical left edge. The turntable rotates continuously on a continuous revolute bearing.

category_title: Wall Safe With Hinged Door And Dial
prompt: A document wall safe with a wide rectangular opening, a left-hinged front door, a round dial, and a shallow inner drawer behind the threshold. The main door rotates on a vertical hinge at the left jamb, the dial rotates continuously about its center shaft, and the inner drawer translates prismatically on straight runners inside the safe body.

A conductor-style orchestra music stand with a broad desk, a single pedestal mast, and folding page supports at the left and right edges. The desk tilts on a horizontal hinge at the mast head, the mast extends prismatically from the pedestal sleeve, and the two page supports rotate on small side pivots.

A rectangular telescoping boom for lifting or inspection equipment. Nested box-section stages extend from a fixed root mount, with a small head bracket at the tip. Each stage slides prismatically along the boom axis within the stage that contains it.

A mountain artillery gun with a wide rectangular split-trail carriage, a round barrel on trunnions, and two large spoked wheels. The barrel pivots on two revolute trunnion joints at the carriage top to elevate. Each wheel rotates continuously about its axle hub.

A compact thermostat with a shallow square body, one front dial, and a flip-down button panel under the display. The front dial rotates continuously about the center axis, and the button panel rotates downward on a lower hinge to reveal the controls behind it. Home wall-control scale.

An exposed three-shaft spur train. A grounded base frame carries input, idler, and output shafts with plain spur gears and visible bearing supports. Each shaft is its own revolute joint about a supported shaft axis.

A campus center four-wing revolving door set flush into a building facade wall. A central post carries four rectangular panel wings. Fixed curved guide walls flank the drum opening. The wing assembly rotates continuously about the central post axis.

A compact office keyboard with a narrow phone shelf and a side power slider. The shallow body carries standard key rows on top, a device slot along the upper edge, and a small switch set into one side wall. The keys move as short prismatic plungers normal to the case top, and the power switch translates prismatically in its short guide slot.

A decorative wheel with a ring truss and small two-person gondolas. The wheel stands between two side frames on a shared base, and the gondolas are suspended from short links around the rim. The wheel rotates continuously on the horizontal center axis, and each gondola rotates on its own small support pivots.

A fold-out drying rack with side wings. A central rack frame carries two wing frames and a lower support frame with hanging rails across the spans. Joints: each wing frame is revolute about its outer hinge line, and the lower support frame folds on its own revolute hinges. Use slimmer rails and a lighter open-frame silhouette.

A narrow broom cupboard with a full-height single door and a hold-open catch. The tall rectangular case is fixed, and the door is mounted on two side hinges with a small latch at the free edge. The door rotates on a vertical hinge axis at the cabinet side, and the latch rotates on its own short pivot.

A pop-up tilt-only sunroof cassette with a flat rectangular glass panel that tilts upward at the rear edge. The glass panel tilts on a revolute hinge at the panel's front edge about a horizontal axis. A wind deflector strip at the front edge lifts on a revolute hinge when the panel tilts.

A side-wall XZ module. A rigid grounded side support carries a lower slide and a narrow vertical stage attached to the lower carriage. The assembly uses one horizontal and one vertical prismatic joint.

Image-stabilized binoculars with two cylindrical objective barrels on a fixed bridge and an internal stabilizing prism block. The bridge hinge adjusts interpupillary distance on a revolute joint. An external focus wheel rotates continuously.

A modular synthesizer rack panel unit with a tall rectangular rack frame and three module panel faces each on a revolute hinge at the panel's vertical left edge. The frame is fixed. Each module panel swings on a revolute hinge for cable access.

A remote-actuated electronic dropper seatpost with a round outer tube clamped in the frame and a round inner tube that telescopes down. The inner tube slides prismatically inside the outer tube. A wireless remote lever at the handlebars pivots on a revolute hinge.

category_title: Sluice gate with vertical lift panel
prompt: A canal-control sluice assembly with a rectangular frame, a vertically guided panel over the channel mouth, and a rising stem passing through a drive head. The panel translates prismatically along the guide direction, the drive handwheel rotates continuously at the top of the stem, and a narrow service hatch in the drive head rotates on a vertical hinge. Civil-infrastructure scale with simple structural members.

A classic flip-top lighter with a rectangular outer case, an exposed perforated chimney insert, and a striker wheel beside the wick. The lid rotates upward on a side-mounted revolute hinge, and the striker wheel spins continuously on a transverse axle between the chimney guards. Pocket-size metal object scale.

A beverage refrigerator with a glazed front door and a side control knob. The tall narrow cabinet has one glass-front door mounted to one side and a small thermostat knob on the inner top frame. The main door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the thermostat knob rotates on its own short shaft.

category_title: Display freezer with sliding glass lids
prompt: A low island freezer with three sliding glass lids, a deep insulated body, and a recessed control area at one corner, plus a temperature-control knob on the end wall. the three lid panels remain distinct framed pieces rather than one large sheet, and the knob remains a separate control in the end panel. All three glass lids slide prismatically along their top rails, and the temperature-control knob rotates continuously on its short shaft. Retail freezer scale.

A harbor lighthouse with a cylindrical lantern cage and an inner light carriage. The lantern room stands above a gallery, and the beacon carriage is supported on a central shaft inside the cage. The beacon carriage rotates continuously about the vertical shaft, and the gallery access gate rotates on a small side hinge.

category_title: Clock tower with rotating hour and minute hands
prompt: A market square clock tower with a square stone shaft, a four-faced clock stage, a bell stage, and a spire. Each clock face has a minute hand and an hour hand, each rotating continuously about the face center coaxial axis.

A snowmobile front ski spindle assembly with a vertical king-post, an A-arm wishbone, and a ski mounting saddle. The A-arm inner ends pivot on revolute joints at the chassis. The ski saddle revolves on a kingpin revolute joint at the outer A-arm end.

A photo spotlight on a rolling tripod base with a telescoping mast, a yoke-mounted head, and a rear handle that folds for carrying. The tripod legs rotate on crown hinges, the mast slides prismatically, the yoke pans on a vertical joint, the head tilts on a horizontal hinge, and the rear handle rotates on two short pivots at the housing. Portable lighting scale.

A windshield wiper assembly with one arm and one blade. A motor housing supports a wiper spindle, a sweep arm, and a blade carrier at the tip. Joints: the arm sweeps on a revolute joint about the spindle axis, and the blade carrier rolls on a second revolute joint along the arm. Spread the joints apart so each axis is easy to read in profile.

A service platform cart with a folding tow handle at the front. The flat deck rides on four swivel casters, and a short tow loop folds from the front edge of the platform. The tow handle rotates on a small hinge at the platform front, and each caster wheel spins and swivels on its supported joints.

A louvered closet shutter door with a lower solid kick panel. The door panel is hung to the jamb on side hinges, with operable louvers above a fixed lower panel. The door rotates on a vertical hinge axis at the jamb, and the louvers rotate on their long-axis pivots within the upper section.

A table tilt-stand box fan with a small square housing on a round tilt-stand base. The housing tilts on a revolute hinge at the stand arms about a horizontal axis. The fan blade rotates continuously. The control knob on the housing side rotates continuously.

A split-palm dual finger mechanism. A rectangular palm block carries left and right phalanx chains on separate root pivots, and each finger ends in a blunt tip pad. Each finger bends through three revolute joints in one plane, and the two fingers stay mechanically independent.

A narrow postbox with a front door and a hinged weather hood over the slot. The tall box body is fixed to a post, and the slot hood sits above the main door on a short hinge. The front door rotates on a vertical hinge axis, and the weather hood rotates on its own small horizontal hinge above the slot.

A bench-top multi-segment fold-out arm. A base foot supports a stack of slim links with clear side plates at the joints, and the distal link ends in a small platform bracket. There are four revolute joints in sequence, each supported by a local hinge block and aligned to the same motion plane.

A compact robotic arm. A base pedestal supports a shoulder joint, an upper arm link, an elbow joint, a forearm link, and a wrist head with a round tool flange. The shoulder rotates about a vertical axis at the pedestal, the elbow rotates about a horizontal axis at the arm knuckle, and the wrist is a revolute joint about the tool axis.

A two-joint revolute chain. A base bracket carries a first rigid link, which carries a second link ending in a small pad. The two joints are serial revolutes about parallel axes in one plane, each with about 90 degrees of travel each way.

A compact speedlight flash with a stepped battery body, a shoe mount, a rectangular lamp head on a two-axis aiming mount, and a side control strip with individual mode buttons. The lower aiming joint rotates about a vertical swivel axis, the upper joint rotates about a horizontal tilt axis supporting the head, and each side control button is a separate prismatic push button mounted in the control strip. Accessory scale with believable panel breaks, shoe-lock geometry, and a realistic head-to-body proportion.

A five-burner gas hob with a central wok burner, five front knobs, and a slim glass splash lid behind the grate area. The splash lid rotates upward on a rear hinge line at the back of the cooking field, and the five front knobs rotate continuously on their short axes. Domestic cooktop scale.

An extended ATX full-tower with a tall rectangular body, a hinged side panel, a swing-out drive cage, and a hinged top exhaust cover. The main side panel opens on revolute hinges at the panel's rear edge. The drive cage pivots on a revolute hinge inside the case. The top exhaust cover opens on a revolute hinge.

A pair of aviator glasses with a double bridge and slim metal temples. The thin front frame carries two adjustable nose-pad arms and slender temple arms attached by compact hinge blocks. The temples fold on revolute hinges at the outer corners, and the two nose pads rotate on small support-arm pivots near the bridge.

A lift-slide arm with a vertical guide carriage, a hinged center plate, and a short extending probe at the tip. The geometry should feel compact at the root and progressively smaller toward the probe. The root carriage is a prismatic axis on the mast, the center plate is a revolute joint on the carriage, and the probe is a distal prismatic joint through the tip housing.

A tall pedestal joystick with a rectangular base housing, an open cardan yoke, an inner frame, and a long tapered control lever. The support structure should read as robust hardware with a clear fixed base, a moving outer yoke, and a second moving inner cradle. Use realistic details like trunnion caps, guard plates, interface flanges, and a grounded lever socket so the control feels like a real machine subassembly. One revolute joint pitches the outer yoke about a transverse axis, and a second revolute joint rolls the inner frame about a perpendicular axis. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

Design a retrofit/legacy-inspired Windshield sun visor that blends old-school construction cues with modern reliability. Keep the core as a visor panel on a roof hinge arm plus secondary swing pivot; include service hatches, bolted adapters, and pragmatic reinforcements where components meet. Primary and secondary pivots should be visible and mechanically credible. The final model must be one connected mechanism where every element is structurally supported. No floating parts.

A searchlight tower with a pan-tilt lamp head. A mast support carries a yoke, and the lamp body sits between the yoke arms. Joints: the head pans on a vertical revolute base and tilts on a horizontal revolute yoke axis. Favor large bearing housings and thick side supports around the rotary stage.

A playground swing with a slatted seat and a hinged lap bar. The seat hangs from two side hanger links under the top beam, and a short lap bar is mounted at the front corners. The seat swings on the hanger pivots at the beam, and the lap bar rotates on its side hinges at the seat edges.

An airliner turbofan with a fold-down access panel under the fan case. The nacelle encloses the fan and core, and a small rectangular panel is mounted on a lower hinge under the intake section. The fan rotor spins continuously about the longitudinal axis, and the lower access panel rotates on its hinge line beneath the fan case.

A retractable-blade ceiling fan with a round motor housing and three blades that fold back into the housing when stopped. Each blade pivots on a revolute hinge at the blade root. When running, the full blade-and-hub assembly rotates continuously.

A benchtop monocular laboratory microscope with a heavy foot, a vertical arm, a straight observation tube, and a rectangular stage with spring clips. The observation tube carriage slides prismatically along a vertical guide on the arm, the objective nosepiece rotates on the optical-axis turret joint, and a pair of coaxial focus knobs rotate on the same horizontal shaft at the arm sides.

A cold-press slow juicer with a wide rectangular base, a large hopper funnel on top, and an auger chamber. The hopper lid opens on a revolute hinge at the lid's back edge. The slow-turning auger rotates continuously about a horizontal axis.

A timber overshot waterwheel with deep bucket paddles and a top feed trough. The wheel is carried by side frames on a central axle, and a hinged inspection flap sits at the end of the feed trough. The wheel rotates continuously on its horizontal axle, and the inspection flap rotates on a small hinge at the trough end.

A hand syringe. A cylindrical barrel holds a plunger rod and plunger head, ending in a nozzle tip. Joints: the plunger translates prismatically along the barrel axis. Keep the moving member tightly nested inside the fixed guide.

A horizontal-axis wind turbine. A tower supports a nacelle and a front hub with multiple blades. Joints: the rotor spins continuously about the main shaft axis, and the nacelle yaws about the vertical tower axis. Make the rotating member large relative to the fixed support.

A celestial globe in an open two-post cradle on a turntable base. The sphere mounts between top and bottom polar pivots, and the cradle stands on a round rotating base plate. The sphere rotates continuously about its polar axis, and the cradle rotates about a vertical axis through the turntable base.

A triple-dial candy vending machine with three stacked bins in a tall body, separate rotary selectors, and one lower retrieval door. The three selector dials rotate continuously on parallel horizontal shafts, and the retrieval door rotates downward on a bottom horizontal hinge.

A book-scanning flatbed with a rectangular body, a wide hinged lid on two barrel revolute hinges at the rear edge, and each hinge incorporating a secondary riser arm on an additional revolute joint at the hinge knuckle to lift the lid high enough to accommodate open thick books.

An electric adult folding commuter scooter with a wide deck, a folding handlebar stem, a front wheel with motor, and a rear fender with a brake pad. The handlebar stem folds at the deck neck on a revolute fold hinge. The stem also telescopes prismatically for height. The front and rear wheels rotate continuously.

A two-tier lazy Susan with a fixed central post and two separate rotating shelves. Each circular shelf is carried by its own bearing collar around the post, so the upper and lower tiers can move independently. The lower shelf rotates on one vertical revolute axis around the post, and the upper shelf rotates on a second vertical revolute axis on its own collar.

An articulated TV wall mount shown without a screen. A grounded wall plate supports a folding two-link arm ending in a compact head frame with separate swivel and tilt supports. The arm has two folding revolute joints, and the head adds one swivel revolute about vertical and one tilt revolute about horizontal.

category_title: Laptop stand with articulated height adjustment
prompt: A travel laptop stand with a thin base panel, a single rigid support platform, and a folding rear support leg beneath the platform. The platform is hinged near its front edge to the base and the rear leg props it at different working heights. The platform rotates on a transverse front hinge, and the support leg rotates on pivots at the base and platform.

A cinema 35mm T1.5 prime with a focus-marking barrel ring, a wide cylindrical body, and a PL locking ring. The focus-marking barrel rotates on a revolute joint about the optical axis. The PL locking ring rotates on a revolute joint to lock the mount.

A USB drive with a swivel cover. A compact body carries the connector, and a U-shaped cover wraps around the body on a side pin. Joints: the cover rotates continuously about the side pivot axis. Keep the axis hardware tucked into the body.

A stick vacuum with a folding wand. A motor body connects to a straight wand and a floor head, with the fold joint placed between the body and the wand. Joints: the fold joint is revolute about a horizontal axis, and the floor head pitches on a second revolute hinge. Make the moving chain long and slender, with clear separation between joints.

A video astronomy tracking telescope with a short fat imaging Newtonian on a motorized single-arm alt-az mount. The telescope tube clamps in a vixen saddle on a revolute altitude axis. The arm rotates on a continuous azimuth motor bearing on a flat tripod head.

A pro-canopy range hood with a tall stainless body, one large center knob, two side buttons, and twin hinged grease filters below the intake. The center knob rotates continuously on its front-facing axis, the two side buttons depress independently as small prismatic controls, and the twin grease filters rotate downward on their rear hinge lines. Large residential kitchen scale.

A benchtop pillar drill press with a round column on a flat rectangular base, a height-adjustable round table, and a three-handle quill feed head. The table slides prismatically up the column. The quill drops prismatically inside the head.

A compact recording camcorder with a front lens cluster, a rear eyecup, and a broad side display panel housed in a thin door. The display door rotates outward on a vertical revolute hinge, the front zoom collar rotates continuously about the viewing axis, and a battery-release latch rotates on a small pivot at the back of the body. Small real-world electronics scale.

A travel-size rice cooker with a squat housing, a hinged top lid, and a small button cluster near the front base, plus a single cook-warm selector dial on the front. the body remains small enough to sit beside a kettle or toaster on a countertop, and the dial sits below the latch rather than floating in the shell. The lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge, and the selector dial rotates continuously about its central axle. Small-appliance scale.

category_title: Pedal trash bin with hinged lid
prompt: A rectangular kitchen pedal bin with a flat-topped lid, a front pedal plate, and a simple inner-bucket handle visible under the lid line, plus a soft-close damper cover on the hinge side. the outer shell and lid keep a clean seam around the top opening, and the damper cover sits as a separate cap over the rear hinge mechanism. The lid rotates on a rear hinge and the pedal plate rotates on a lower front pivot, and the damper cover rotates on a short hinge at the back. Household waste-bin scale.

A yaw-pitch-roll wrist module. A grounded base yoke carries a yaw stage, the yaw stage carries a pitch cradle, and the pitch cradle carries a compact roll cartridge with a small flange at the tip. The wrist uses three serial revolute joints: yaw about vertical, pitch about a horizontal cross-axis, and roll about the tool axis.

A track bike fork with a round carbon steerer and a set of deep-drop track handlebars on a quill stem. The quill stem inserts into the steerer on a prismatic joint for height adjustment. The handlebars clamp in the stem on a revolute joint for angle.

A folding A-frame step ladder. A front climbing frame and a rear support frame meet at a top cap, with wide treads on the front frame. Joints: the rear frame folds relative to the front frame on a single revolute hinge at the top cap. Use a lower wider stance with the supports spread farther apart.

A robotic leg module for a walking machine. An upper leg housing carries a thigh link, shank link, and compact ankle-foot section. Joints: hip revolute about a lateral axis, knee revolute about a lateral axis, ankle revolute about a lateral axis. Split the fixed root into two side plates or cheeks framing the first moving member.

An upright casino machine with a rectangular cabinet, a three-reel viewing window, a handle at the right side, and a lockable coin tray door at the base. The reels rotate continuously on parallel horizontal axes, the side handle rotates on a revolute pivot at the cabinet wall, and the coin tray door rotates downward on a bottom horizontal hinge.

A medical room air purifier with a wide rectangular housing on a stand base, a top-mounted HEPA access lid, and an internal pre-filter that slides out the side. The top lid opens on a revolute hinge at the rear lid edge. The side pre-filter panel slides prismatically on side guide rails.

A heavy-duty utility knife with a rotating lock wheel and a sliding blade carrier. The broad handle body supports the internal carriage and a round lock wheel near the front side. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the lock wheel rotates on its own short shaft in the side wall.

A trumpet case with a rectangular body and a top-opening lid. The lower shell is boxy and shallow, and the broad lid is mounted at the back edge with two front latches and a central handle. The lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge axis, and the handle rotates on its two local pivots at the front face.

A power-meter crankset with a bottom-bracket shell, two hollow-forged crank arms, a single chainring, and a strain-gauge spider. The crank assembly rotates continuously. Each clipless pedal rotates continuously about its axle and also tilts on a revolute float joint about the pedal's long axis.

A tabletop portal gantry with two side uprights, a spanning top beam, a carriage traveling along that beam, and a Z slide hanging from the carriage. The frame should feel like an automation bridge with clear guide paths on the beam and on the hanging slide. The carriage is a horizontal prismatic axis along the beam, and the hanging ram is a vertical prismatic axis on the carriage.

A locking cat flap with a side sliding security panel. The fixed frame surrounds the top-hinged flap panel, and a narrow lockout panel slides in guides along one side of the opening. The flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and the security panel slides prismatically in its side guides.

A three-wheel knee scooter with one front steering wheel and two rear support wheels. The rigid frame carries the knee pad above the center, the front wheel sits in a steerable fork, and the rear wheels mount on a fixed axle spread behind the pad. The front fork yaws about the steering axis, and the front and rear wheels spin on their axle supports. Keep the single front wheel clipped between the fork legs so the steering unit remains supported through yaw motion.

A greenhouse vent window with a top-hinged sash and short side scissor stays. The glazed sash fits inside a light rectangular frame, and each side stay links the sash to the jamb to support partial opening. The sash rotates outward on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and each scissor stay rotates on its paired pivots between the sash and the frame. Keep each scissor stay clipped to both the sash and the frame so the support arms remain attached during venting motion.

An edge-of-dock lip plate leveler with a flat rectangular deck and a pivoting lip extension plate at the front edge. The lip plate pivots on a revolute hinge at the front edge of the deck to bridge the gap to the truck bed. The deck itself pivots on a revolute hinge at the dock edge.

A home automation hub controller with a circular disc body and a rotating outer ring for device selection. The outer ring rotates continuously about the central vertical axis. A rectangular battery door on the underside slides prismatically.

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A hub-and-spoke two-branch motion rig. A vertical mast carries two short spoke arms on discrete hub blocks, and each hub block is visibly larger than the arm it supports. The spoke arms are independent revolute joints about supported hub axes.

A three-lever linkage with a grounded base lug, a curved input lever, a short coupler lever, and a long output lever ending in a narrow tab. The bars should feel like stiff plate parts with pinned joints and just enough offset to avoid reading as a flat sketch. Make it read like real linkage hardware with machined pins, washer stacks, formed lever edges, and believable thickness around the root bracket. All three articulations are revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A service arm module with an extendable end stage. A fixed support carries an upper link and a forelink, and the forelink supports a short guided slider that stays visibly smaller than the main links. The arm uses shoulder and elbow revolute joints plus a terminal prismatic joint.

A keg hand truck with a curved back frame and a folding retaining hook. The tall frame has two transport wheels at the rear and a small top hook that folds down over the load. The rear wheels spin on their axle, and the retaining hook rotates on a small hinge near the top of the frame.

A washer with a top-access service panel — a white rectangular body, a drum on a continuous revolute axle, a circular porthole door on the left edge on two barrel revolute knuckle hinges, and a hinged top service panel on two rear revolute hinge pins for access to the control board.

A rounded food processor with a low motor base, a medium clear bowl, two side latch arms, and a rear-hinged lid with a feed pusher at the center. The lid rotates upward on a rear hinge, the pusher slides prismatically in the chute, the two side latch arms rotate on matched side pivots, and the blade carrier rotates continuously on the bowl centerline. Two control dials on the front face rotate continuously on their own axes. The bowl and lid should remain visibly hollow with open prep volume rather than reading as solid bubbles. A utility blimp with a cigar-shaped envelope, a structural gondola hung below the centerline, twin engine nacelles, and a cruciform tail with distinct control surfaces. A portable rolling tool chest with a ribbed plastic shell, a telescoping trolley handle at the back, and a front organizer lid on the top tray. A desk fan with a tilting head. A base supports a guarded fan head between two side brackets. Joints: the fan rotor spins continuously about its axle, and the head tilts on a horizontal revolute axis through the side brackets. Favor large bearing housings and thick side supports around the rotary stage.

A heavy remote weapon station with a rotating base and a dual-side elevation fork. The lower ring carries a rigid fork, and the weapon housing sits between the fork arms with a small top access hatch. The base rotates on a vertical axis, the weapon housing rotates on a horizontal axis between the fork arms, and the access hatch rotates on a short top hinge.

A dark-finish stand mixer with a chunky rear neck, a broad bowl on a small carriage, and a compact head carrying a flat beater. A compact pickup tailgate with twin top latch knobs and a fold-flat work stop. The outer gate is mounted at the lower edge of the bed, and a slim work-stop bar folds from the top edge of the inner panel. The tailgate rotates on the lower horizontal hinge axis, the two latch knobs rotate on their own short shafts, and the work-stop bar rotates on a small hinge at the top edge.

A wall-mount fold-flat display easel with a rectangular wall plate and a fold-out tilting panel arm. The panel arm folds on a revolute hinge at the top edge of the wall plate. A leg brace locks the arm at the display angle on a revolute hinge.

A tower-mounted pitch-roll unit. A short pedestal supports a compact two-axis head whose outer member is visibly larger than the inner output cradle. The outer member rolls on a revolute joint and the inner cradle pitches on a perpendicular revolute joint.

An undershot waterwheel. Two side frames support a horizontal axle carrying a paddle wheel below a simple channel or trough edge. Joints: the wheel rotates continuously about the axle axis. Favor large bearing housings and thick side supports around the rotary stage.

A naval-style box missile launcher with a rotating pedestal, a tilting canister frame, and two protective blast covers over the front of the canisters. The pedestal rotates on a vertical revolute axis, the canister frame elevates on a horizontal revolute axis, and each blast cover rotates on its own hinge along the canister front.

A modern centrifugal juicer with a low oval base, a clear upper chamber, and a rectangular pusher fitted to a wide feed chute. A short selector lever sits on the front of the base, and a narrow drip drawer is recessed below the juice outlet. The pusher slides prismatically along the chute guide, the chamber lid rotates on a rear hinge, the selector lever rotates on a short front pivot, the drip drawer slides prismatically out from the lower body, and the internal basket rotates continuously about the vertical motor axis.

A pyramid metronome with two independently adjustable weights on the pendulum rod for fine and coarse tempo control. A tapered wooden housing on a rectangular base carries a vertical pendulum rod above the top; two cylindrical weights of different sizes ride on the rod at different heights. Pendulum swings on a revolute at the housing-top shaft; each weight slides prismatically along the rod independently; winding key on the rear face rotates continuously.

An upright tower router with four thin fin antennas and a front rocker switch. The vertical slab body stands on a small base, and two antennas mount near the top on each side through short pivot pods. The four antennas rotate on their individual hinge axes, and the rocker switch rotates on its own small axis near the lower front edge. A traditional windmill with a rotating sail hub. A tower body supports a cap and a front hub carrying multiple lattice blades. Joints: the blade hub rotates continuously about the windmill shaft axis, and the cap rotates about the vertical tower axis. Use a low wide support with the rotating mass kept near the ground or mounting plane.

A bridge-backed monitor mount. A rigid rear frame carries a lower arm, an upper arm, and a compact display plate, with the head hardware clearly smaller than the arm structure. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints, and the head has one swivel revolute plus one tilt revolute.

A battery-powered speaker with a rounded rectangular shell, a folding carry strap at the top, and a control panel with a central dial, plus a power rocker and two menu buttons. the control cluster is clearly separate from the speaker grille area, and the rocker stays distinct from the two smaller buttons beside it. The strap rotates on two small side pivots and the central dial rotates continuously on its local axis, and the power rocker rotates on a short pivot and the two menu buttons each depress independently. Small outdoor-speaker scale.

A service-station pump with a tubular barrel, a straight T-handle, and a side-mounted gauge housing above the foot pads, plus a folding handle grip around the top bar. the hose remains clipped to the lower body when not in use, and the grip remains a supported sleeve on the crossbar. The handle and piston rod translate prismatically along the pump axis and the gauge needle rotates continuously on its local pivot, and the grip rotates continuously about the crossbar axis. Service-bay pump scale.

category_title: Dishwasher with dropdown door and sliding racks
prompt: A pro-style stainless dishwasher with a deep dropdown door, a tall upper rack, a broad lower rack, and a front control fascia carrying a large cycle knob, a start button, and a delayed-start button. A three-joint robotic arm for pick-and-place work. A pedestal base carries a shoulder housing, upper arm link, forearm link, and compact wrist head. A stationary exercise bike. A floor frame supports the flywheel housing, crank set, seat post, and handlebar mast. Joints: the crank rotates continuously about the bottom-bracket axis, the flywheel rotates continuously about its axle, and the seat post adjusts prismatically along its column. Place the wheels close to the body so the silhouette stays tight.

A long-range cinematic folding quadcopter with four folding arms and a three-axis camera stabilizer gimbal. Each arm folds on a revolute hinge at the arm root. The gimbal has a pan continuous joint, a tilt revolute joint, and a roll revolute joint stacked. Each propeller rotates continuously.

A C-clamp portable vise with a C-shaped steel body, a fixed upper jaw, and a lower screw-driven jaw pad. The screw drives the lower jaw pad prismatically upward along the C-frame axis. The screw handle rotates continuously.

A commuter kick scooter with a rear swingarm suspension and a folding kickstand. The rigid deck carries the front steer tube at the nose, while the rear wheel is mounted in a short swingarm hinged under the tail and a side stand folds below the deck. The front fork yaws about the steering axis, both wheels spin on their axles, the rear swingarm rotates on its transverse pivot under the deck, and the kickstand rotates on its small hinge mount. Keep the rear swingarm clipped to its pivot under the deck so the wheel support stays attached through suspension travel.

category_title: Top-load washing machine with hinged lid
prompt: A family top-load washer with a glass lid, a wide drum opening, and two flip-up dispenser lids set into the rear corners of the top deck behind a selector knob and a start button. category_title: Desktop pencil sharpener with hand crank and drawer

category_title: Adjustable weight bench with hinged backrest
prompt: A commercial adjustable bench with a wide rear support arm, a split pad layout, and a heavy welded frame with small transport wheels, plus a pop-pin angle selector under the backrest. the support arm is clearly separate from the main backrest plate, and the selector knob is visibly separate from the support arm. The backrest pivots on a transverse hinge above the main frame, the seat pivots on a forward hinge, the wide rear support arm rotates on a lower frame pivot, and the transport wheels spin continuously on their axle mounts, and the pop-pin knob translates prismatically along its short local axis. A travel over-ear headphone set with folding cups and telescoping headband sliders. Each earcup hangs from a yoke with a fold hinge, and the yokes slide in and out of the headband arms for size adjustment. Each cup swivels on its yoke axis, each side folds on a revolute hinge above the cup, and each headband slider moves prismatically along its arm.

A flush dashboard glove box with an upper-hinged door and a small rotary latch knob. The storage tub is fixed behind the fascia, and the door panel is supported along its top edge with compact side friction stays behind it. The door rotates upward on a horizontal upper hinge axis, the latch knob rotates on its own small axis near the lower edge, and the side stays rotate on their support pivots as the door lifts. Keep the upper-hinged door clipped to the top hinge line so the panel stays captured as the friction stays move.

A dual-slot desktop graphics card. A long PCB carries a shrouded heatsink and two axial fans on the front face, with an I/O bracket at one short end. Each fan rotor is supported in its own circular frame and rotates continuously about a central revolute axis normal to the card face.

A heat-pump tumble dryer with a white rectangular body, a drum on a central continuous revolute axle, a porthole door on the left edge on two barrel revolute hinges with a chrome ring bezel, and a heat-pump filter access drawer on a prismatic joint at the plinth base.

A floor ring light with a rolling base and a tilting light head. The circular light sits in a yoke at the top of a central mast, and the mast rises from a low caster base. The light head rotates on a horizontal tilt axis, the caster wheels spin on their axles and swivel on their stems, and the mast slides prismatically for height adjustment.

A toy pickup truck with a boxy cab, an open cargo bed, a front hood, and four large wheels. The four wheels rotate continuously on their axles, the hood rotates upward on a rear horizontal hinge at the cowl, and the tailgate rotates on a lower horizontal hinge at the back of the bed.

A countertop waffle maker with a cast lower cooking base, a domed upper lid on a rear hinge, a front indicator panel, and separate power and ready buttons beside a browning dial. The upper lid rotates upward on a rear horizontal revolute hinge, the browning dial rotates continuously about its center axis, and each visible front-panel button is a separate prismatic push button. Compact kitchen-appliance scale with a realistic front handle, hinge cover, and molded side cheeks.

A small office microwave with a flush door, two stacked dials, and a slim door-open key. The fixed cavity sits behind the front opening, and the glass turntable is centered low inside the oven chamber. The door rotates on a vertical hinge axis at one side, the turntable rotates continuously on its spindle, the two dials rotate on their local axes, and the door-open key translates prismatically in its guide slot. Keep the slim door-open key clipped in its guide slot so it translates without leaving the front panel.

A dual-screen flip phone with a lower body and an upper body each carrying a flat display face, joined by a thin flat folding hinge on a revolute joint that keeps both screen faces parallel at every opening angle.

A small propeller model airplane. A fuselage carries fixed wings and tail, with a nose propeller and a simple landing gear or display peg under the belly. Joints: the propeller spins continuously about the nose axis. Use paired flanges or cheeks to define the rotating support zone.

A kamado-style ceramic grill on a wheeled nest stand. The thick lower bowl sits in a tubular cradle with two wheels, the domed lid is hinged at the rear, and a daisy-wheel vent cap sits on top of the dome. The lid rotates upward on a rear revolute hinge axis, the two transport wheels spin on their axle mounts, and the vent cap rotates on its central fastener at the lid crown. Keep the domed lid clipped to the rear hinge hardware so the heavy upper shell stays supported through opening.

category_title: Palm with multiple uncoupled finger chains
prompt: A mechanical palm plate with three forward finger chains and one side thumb chain for a simple gripper hand. The palm is a flat plate with separate knuckle mounts, and each digit is a short serial chain of rigid phalanx-like links connected end to end. Each digit root rotates on its own pivot at the palm, and each additional joint in the digit chains rotates on its own local hinge axis with no coupling between digits. Keep each digit chain clipped together at its knuckle pivots so the links stay attached instead of separating during curl motion.

A heavy paper cutter with a blade arm, a sliding side gauge, and a hinged transparent finger guard near the cut line. The base carries the gauge rail and the guard panel, while the blade arm mounts through a corner pivot bracket. The blade arm rotates on its main revolute hinge, the side gauge slides prismatically along its rail, and the guard panel rotates on a small hinge above the cutting edge.

A compact education laptop with a slightly thicker bezel and a side power slider. The lower chassis supports the keyboard deck, the display lid mounts on two rear hinges, and a small switch sits in a guide slot on one side wall. The lid rotates on the rear hinge axis, the keys move as short vertical prismatic plungers, and the power switch translates prismatically in its side slot. Keep the side power slider clipped in its guide slot so it translates without separating from the side wall.

category_title: Lounge chair with independent backrest and footrest
prompt: A slim lounge chair with a wood side frame, a reclining back, and a telescoping calf rest under the seat. The seat base is fixed between the side frames, the back panel pivots at the rear of the seat, and a rectangular footrest board slides forward from guides below the front rail. The backrest rotates on a horizontal rear pivot axis, and the footrest slides prismatically forward and back on its guide rails independently of the back. A low-profile sandwich press with a rectangular shell, a top platen that can rest at variable height, and a short carry handle along the front edge. The top platen rotates upward on a back-edge revolute hinge, a side height-stop lever rotates on a small revolute pivot to set the open angle, and a selector knob rotates continuously on the side of the base. An ironing board with a folding leg trestle. A board top mounts on a base bracket, with one U-shaped leg frame under the middle and a small fixed rest at one end. Joints: the main leg frame folds on a horizontal revolute axis under the board. Use a lower wider stance with the supports spread farther apart.

Design Trigger spray bottle as a field-service workhorse. Build it around a bottle plus trigger head with visible trigger-to-pump linkage. Prioritize chunky hardware, replaceable wear parts, clear maintenance access, and robust tolerances for repeated use. Trigger stroke should transmit through a visible lever-and-pump path. Every part must be connected by real brackets, shafts, hinges, rails, collars, weldments, or housings; absolutely no floating geometry. No floating parts.

A hammerhead saddle-jib tower crane with a square lattice mast, a horizontal saddle jib, a counter jib carrying a concrete counterweight, and a hook trolley that slides along the jib. The saddle jib rotates continuously about the mast top vertical axis. The trolley slides prismatically along the jib bottom chord. The hook line block is fixed below the trolley.

A sliding compound miter saw assembly with twin horizontal rails. The base carries a turntable and fence, while the saw head rides on a carriage that slides fore-aft on the rails and also pivots downward from the rail carriage. The saw head pitches down on a transverse revolute axis through about 50 degrees, the carriage slides prismatically along the rail axis about 200 mm, and the turntable yaws on a vertical revolute axis about +/-45 degrees.

A soap dispenser pump bottle. A cylindrical bottle body supports a threaded neck with a pump collar, a vertical plunger stem, and a short spout head at the top. The plunger moves as a vertical prismatic slider with short downward travel, and the spout head may rotate continuously about the stem axis.

category_title: Digital multimeter with tilt stand and rotary selector
prompt: A rugged autoranging digital multimeter with a boxy main body, a broad LCD bezel, a central range dial, and a rear kickstand with a shallow angle stop. The range dial rotates continuously on the front face, the kickstand hinges from the lower back of the case, and the hold, min-max, and backlight buttons each move independently as prismatic push controls below the screen. A pedestal basin faucet with a tall chrome column body fixed to the basin and a side lever handle. The lever handle rotates on a revolute joint about a horizontal axis at the valve body.

A compact linear adjustment module with a thick base plate, a short guide rail, and a sliding clamp block carrying a small top pad. The sliding block should clearly belong to the rail and the rail should clearly belong to the base, with no ambiguous floating interfaces. Use realistic detail such as rail caps, clamp screws, end stops, and practical carriage wall thickness around the guide overlap. One prismatic joint translates the clamp block along the rail axis. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A pedestal spindle shaft module. A grounded pedestal frame supports a horizontal spindle through two spaced supports and a plain end flange. The spindle is a single continuous joint about its supported shaft axis.

A survey-head style yaw module. A grounded pedestal carries a compact rotary head and a narrow rectangular instrument pad above it. One revolute joint turns the instrument pad about the pedestal axis.

category_title: Sewing box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A small sewing box with a plain top lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Make the hinge hardware short and tucked close to the body.

A refrigerated laboratory sample cabinet with an insulated rectangular body and four wide stainless drawers. Each drawer slides prismatically on full-extension steel slides. A hinged access panel on the rear top opens on a revolute hinge.

A medical exam-room lamp on a ceiling two-link articulated arm. A ceiling plate connects to a shoulder revolute joint; the upper arm links to an elbow revolute; the forearm ends in a round lamp head on a revolute pan joint and a revolute tilt joint at the yoke.

A single-sash sliding window. A rectangular outer frame contains one fixed frame segment and one sliding sash. Joints: the sash translates prismatically along horizontal guide channels. Use a cleaner rail layout with fewer visible guide parts.

A side-plate roll-axis spindle module. A grounded side wall carries a forward-projecting axis cartridge and a small carried faceplate, keeping the support path explicit from plate to output. One revolute joint spins the output flange about its own longitudinal shaft axis between fixed supports.

A boxed cover hinge. A fixed bracket leaf carries short knuckle segments and a moving cover leaf with a simple edge flange. The cover leaf rotates as one revolute joint about the supported barrel axis.

A pedestal bar stool with a contoured seat, a circular base, and a short fold-out step at the post. The seat rotates continuously on the vertical pedestal axis, and the fold-out step rotates on a short hinge at the post. Counter-height seating scale.

A dual-column lift carriage. Two vertical guide columns are tied together by a grounded base and top crosshead, while a rectangular carriage spans between them with obvious guide sleeves or blocks. The carriage is one prismatic joint moving along the vertical column axis.

A split-level XY stage. A grounded lower slide carries a long first carriage, and that carriage supports a shorter crosswise second slide with a plain top plate. The lower and upper slides are orthogonal horizontal prismatic joints.

A compact forklift-style mast module. Two upright channels and a top crosshead support a moving fork carriage, with the forks rigid to the front of the carriage. One prismatic joint lifts the carriage along the mast axis.

A vane array with independent pivots. A grounded frame carries a row of rigid slats or vanes, each supported at its ends by simple side brackets or pivots. Each vane is its own revolute joint about its long pivot axis, and the vanes remain mechanically independent.

A screw-in light bulb seated in a socket. A bulb envelope sits above a threaded metal base, and the socket body holds the mating threaded collar. Joints: the bulb rotates continuously about the shared socket axis. Make the rotating element narrow and long.

A record turntable. A plinth supports a central platter and a tonearm base off to one side. Joints: the platter rotates continuously about vertical, and the tonearm swings on a revolute pivot at the tonearm base. Make the rotating member large relative to the fixed support.

A low-profile three-stage slide module. A broad outer stage supports a shorter middle stage and a compact inner stage, keeping each nested level easy to read. The moving stages are serial prismatic joints on the shared guide axis.

A bridge-backed slide with two-link arm. A rear frame supports a linear carriage, and the carriage supports a compact two-link arm ending in a plain pad. The carriage is prismatic and the two arm breaks are revolute joints.

A dual-rail gantry axis. Two parallel grounded base rails support a bridge carriage, and a smaller truck rides across that bridge on its own guide path. The bridge is one prismatic joint along the base rails, and the truck is a second prismatic joint along the crossbeam.

A skateboard with standard trucks. A deck supports front and rear truck hangers, each holding one axle and two wheels. Joints: each wheel spins continuously about its axle, and each truck steers on a revolute kingpin axis. Place the wheels close to the body so the silhouette stays tight.

A double-leaf decorative driveway swing gate with two equal rectangular ornamental frame panels, each on a pair of pintle hinges at opposite pillars. The left panel revolves on two revolute joints at the left pillar. The right panel revolves on two revolute joints at the right pillar.

A laboratory workbench desk with a thick top, a bank of three locking drawers in a side pedestal, and a pull-out keyboard shelf. Each drawer slides prismatically on guide rails. The keyboard shelf extends prismatically from its under-surface runners.

A USB drive with a swivel cover. A compact body carries the connector, and a U-shaped cover wraps around the body on a side pin. Joints: the cover rotates continuously about the side pivot axis. Use one simple sleeve-like fixed support around the axis.

An aero road bike fork with an integrated stem and a set of narrow drop handlebars. The fork steerer tube is fixed inside the head tube; the integrated stem is fixed to the steerer top. The handlebar rotates on a continuous revolute joint about the stem clamp axis for angle adjustment.

A folding A-frame step ladder. A front climbing frame and a rear support frame meet at a top cap, with wide treads on the front frame. Joints: the rear frame folds relative to the front frame on a single revolute hinge at the top cap. Use a taller open stance with a narrow support footprint.

A fixie track crankset with a solid right crank arm, a left arm, and a single keyed track chainring. The spindle and both crank arms rotate continuously. Each pedal rotates continuously about its axle.

A tabletop portal axis with a vertical ram. A fixed portal frame carries a moving crosshead on the beam, and the crosshead carries a compact vertical quill plate. The crosshead and quill are separate prismatic joints on horizontal and vertical guide directions.

A narrow fork carriage module with two upright C-channels, a top crosshead, a load backrest, and short pallet forks projecting from a compact carriage. A compact service vent with rigid blades. A grounded perimeter frame carries several thickened blades with simple end pivots and clear spacing between them. Each blade is an independent revolute joint.

A screw-in light bulb seated in a socket. A bulb envelope sits above a threaded metal base, and the socket body holds the mating threaded collar. Joints: the bulb rotates continuously about the shared socket axis. Use a cleaner silhouette with fewer secondary breaks around the rotating part.

A record turntable. A plinth supports a central platter and a tonearm base off to one side. Joints: the platter rotates continuously about vertical, and the tonearm swings on a revolute pivot at the tonearm base. Use a lighter open support with the rotary stage exposed.

An under-slung coaxial stage stack. A top support and center shaft carry multiple concentric stages below, giving the stack a hanging silhouette. The concentric stages rotate as separate revolute joints about one shared axis.

A bench module with a slide and a two-link arm. A fixed guide body supports a carriage-mounted shoulder block, then an upper link, then a short distal link with a square pad. The carriage is prismatic, the shoulder is a revolute joint on the carriage, and the elbow is a second revolute joint on the arm.

A bridge-rider dual-rail gantry. Two grounded rails support a moving bridge, and the bridge carries a compact crosswise truck on its own guide path. The bridge and the truck are stacked prismatic joints on perpendicular axes.

A skateboard with standard trucks. A deck supports front and rear truck hangers, each holding one axle and two wheels. Joints: each wheel spins continuously about its axle, and each truck steers on a revolute kingpin axis. Use slimmer tubular members and a lighter open-frame body.

A bench automation XYZ module. A grounded base axis supports a crosswise second carriage, and the second carriage supports a narrow vertical stage ending in a square output face. The lower, middle, and upper stages are prismatic joints on orthogonal axes.

An opposed twin-slide gripper. A central body carries left and right jaws on mirrored linear slides with guide rails and a fixed mounting plate at the rear. The two jaws are separate prismatic joints along a common closing axis, each with about 40 mm travel toward the center.

A retractable post barrier with a round chrome post that telescopes into a flush ground socket. The post slides prismatically along the vertical axis into the cylindrical socket.

A Japanese rice paper floor lamp with a round base, a tall post, and a large cylindrical drum shade around the post top. The shade slides onto the post with a prismatic friction fit. A pull chain rotates on a continuous revolute joint at a pulley in the housing.

A carillon bell tower with a square stone shaft, an open belfry stage, and five bells of different sizes hung on individual headstocks across the belfry beams. Each bell hangs on its own revolute hinge at its headstock. Each bell swings independently about its own horizontal axis.

A rotary column with lift carriage. A grounded circular base supports a rotating column, and the column supports a compact vertical slide. The column is a revolute joint about the base centerline, and the carried slide is a vertical prismatic joint.

An open-cage industrial goods elevator with a wire-mesh cage car on a vertical post tower. The cage slides prismatically along vertical post guide rails. A hinged cage gate at the car entrance swings on revolute knuckles at the gate's vertical edge.

A boxed industrial three-joint revolute chain. A reinforced base cheek supports deeper-section links with visible pin bosses, and the final link terminates in a compact end tab. The assembly is a serial chain of three revolute joints about parallel hinge axes.

A bridge-backed YZ transfer unit. A fixed vertical plate supports a crosswise beam carriage, and the beam carriage supports a shorter vertical stage below it. The beam carriage is a horizontal prismatic joint and the lower stage is a vertical prismatic joint.

A hinge-and-ram chain. A grounded base bracket supports one rigid arm, and the arm supports a compact slider near its tip. The root is a revolute joint and the distal stage is a prismatic joint along the arm-supported guide.

A compact laptop-style overbed table with a narrow offset base, a slender telescoping column, a rectangular top, and a front retaining lip, plus a smaller reading wing beside the main top. the support head keeps the top centered over the column rather than cantilevering from a hidden arm, and the secondary wing is mounted on a short support bracket at the tray edge. A round stainless-steel three-wing revolving door with mirror-polished drum and wing frames. A central stainless post carries three equal stainless-framed rectangular wings. The wing assembly rotates continuously about the vertical axis.
