Keep it as a pure mechanical assembly with explicit joints, guides, bearings, brackets, and support structure. Avoid product styling, decorative housings, or recognizable household or device forms.

Create a cost-optimized mass-manufacturable Windshield sun visor. Base architecture: a visor panel on a roof hinge arm plus secondary swing pivot. Reduce part count, simplify molds/extrusions/stampings, and keep assembly order obvious with realistic snap/bolt/clamp interfaces. Primary and secondary pivots should be visible and mechanically credible. Every piece must have a believable attachment path; no floating or orphaned components. No floating parts.

Design a retrofit/legacy-inspired Searchlight tower that blends old-school construction cues with modern reliability. A disc-seat swing with a small folding footrest ring. The circular seat hangs from a compact hanger below the top beam, and a narrow ring footrest folds from the underside. The seat swings on the hanger pivot at the beam, and the footrest ring rotates on a small hinge under the seat.

A compact turbojet module on a maintenance stand with a visible compressor face and a short exhaust nozzle. An outdoor wet-rated ceiling fan with a sealed motor housing on a downrod and two wide blade arms with blades. The blade assembly rotates continuously. The downrod mounts to the canopy on a fixed swivel joint.

A benchtop monocular laboratory microscope with a horseshoe base, a curved arm, an inclined monocular tube, and a square mechanical stage. A vacuum blender with a round base housing, a tall cylindrical jug that seats on a revolute twist lock, and a suction connector cap. The jug locks on the base with a revolute twist. A sealing cap on the jug top presses in on a short prismatic fit. The blade rotates continuously.

A masonry-supported overshot waterwheel with a small drive pulley and a chute gate. A hand syringe. A cylindrical barrel holds a plunger rod and plunger head, ending in a nozzle tip. Joints: the plunger translates prismatically along the barrel axis. Use a low wide frame with the moving member spanning most of the opening.

A horizontal-axis wind turbine. A tower supports a nacelle and a front hub with multiple blades. Joints: the rotor spins continuously about the main shaft axis, and the nacelle yaws about the vertical tower axis. Keep the rotating stage compact and close to the support.

A minimalist globe on a canted single-sided axle over a ring base. A pedestal gumball vending machine with a spherical clear bowl, a cast lower mechanism housing, and a hinged refill lid on top. A film-strip slide scanner with a narrow rectangular body, a hinged top lid on two barrel revolute hinges at the rear edge, and a slide film holder tray that slides on a prismatic joint at the front body face for inserting and indexing film strips.

A stairclimber scooter with a flat deck, front and rear primary wheels on revolute axles, and two auxiliary stair-assist wheels at the deck rear corners each on a revolute axle, folding on revolute hinges at the deck underside corner mounts when not in use.

An oval condiment lazy susan on a short pedestal base. The upper tray has a raised outer rim and two small grab handles, and it sits on a central turntable above a fixed lower base. A side-hinge TV wall mount shown without the screen. A grounded wall plate supports two arm links and a compact display head with distinct tilt and swivel support pieces. The arm uses two folding revolute joints, and the head adds one swivel revolute and one tilt revolute.

category_title: Laptop stand with articulated height adjustment

A compact kit zoom with a retracting barrel that collapses when not in use. The inner zoom barrel rotates and telescopes prismatically along the optical axis into the outer barrel. A zoom ring on the outer barrel rotates on a revolute joint.

A USB drive with a swivel cover. A compact body carries the connector, and a U-shaped cover wraps around the body on a side pin. Joints: the cover rotates continuously about the side pivot axis. Break the fixed body into two or three clear coaxial sections.

A stick vacuum with a folding wand. A motor body connects to a straight wand and a floor head, with the fold joint placed between the body and the wand. A short f/5 Newtonian reflector on a motorized equatorial mount. A cylindrical tube body with a prismatic rack-and-pinion focuser draw-tube clamps in a dovetail saddle on a revolute declination axis and a continuous right-ascension axis.

An angled-glass range hood with a sloped front glass panel, a shallow canopy body, and a row of four raised push buttons along the lower edge. A radial arm drill press with a round column, a radial arm that sweeps on the column, and a drill head that slides along the arm. The radial arm rotates continuously on a revolute bearing on the column. The drill head slides prismatically along the arm. The quill drops prismatically.

A documentary-style camcorder with a top handle, a wide front lens hood, and a side viewing screen carried on a narrow hinge arm. A simple rice cooker with a cylindrical body, a domed upper lid, and a small front control panel, plus a folding carry handle spanning the upper shoulders and a power button below the front display. An open-frame yaw-pitch-roll wrist assembly. A fixed root plate, one vertical rotary stage, one horizontal cradle, and one axial roll spindle define the mechanism without enclosing the moving members. The module contains three revolute joints on vertical, horizontal, and tool-axis directions.

A dirt-jump bike fork with a wide stiff alloy crown, two straight round-section legs, and a threadless steerer clamped in a mid-rise stem. A folding A-frame step ladder. A front climbing frame and a rear support frame meet at a top cap, with wide treads on the front frame. A high-top slot machine with a narrow lower body, a tall topper housing, a reel window, and a service door on the front lower section. A small bedside personal air purifier with a round cylindrical tower housing and a twist-off top cap for filter access. The top cap rotates as a revolute joint about the vertical axis to unlock. The internal filter cylinder slides prismatically out from the top opening.

A metal-body retractable utility knife with a top thumb slider and a lock wheel. A portable mixer case with a deep lower shell and a broad lid supported by short stay links. A single-speed flat-bar urban crankset with a round bottom-bracket shell, two crank arms, a single chainring, and two platform pedals. The crankset spindle and both arms rotate continuously. Each pedal rotates continuously about its axle.

An under-beam portal axis. A top beam carries a hanging beam rider, and the rider carries a compact vertical carriage below its center. The beam rider slides on one prismatic joint and the lower carriage slides on a vertical prismatic joint.

A locking pet flap with a rigid clear panel and a hinged exterior weather hood. The flap hangs from the upper frame edge inside the opening, and a short hood is hinged above it on the exterior face. A knee scooter with a front basket, four small wheels, and a fold-down parking stand. A service hatch in a vehicle or equipment body with a gullwing lid. A ground-mounted tilt-deck dock plate with a flat steel platform on a central horizontal revolute hinge at a ground frame, and two support leg columns below the platform each extending on a prismatic joint to set the tilt angle.

A wireless conference room presenter with a flat slim body and a pull-out QWERTY keyboard tray. The keyboard tray extends prismatically from the bottom edge on guide rails. A tilt-stand leg on the back pivots on a revolute hinge at the bottom rear edge.

A compact lever chain. A grounded root bracket carries 4 rigid links in series, using offset pin joints between each link and ending in a small end tab. The chain has three serial revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane.

A twin-tub washing machine with a wide rectangular body divided into two compartments — a left wash drum on a continuous revolute axle and a right spin-dry drum on a separate continuous revolute axle — each with a separate porthole door on its own left-edge revolute hinges.

A classic food processor with a narrow bowl, a rear-hinged lid, a cylindrical feed pusher, and a front control cluster made of a speed knob and two square buttons. A presentation A-frame display easel with two front legs fixed at the top hinge and a rear adjustable brace. The rear brace slides prismatically in a slot on the front-leg frame and locks at the desired angle.

A trunnion-and-spindle pitch-roll unit. A side-supported pitch frame carries a centered roll spindle with a short front face. Pitch is a revolute joint about the frame trunnions, and roll is a separate revolute joint about the spindle axis.

An undershot waterwheel. Two side frames support a horizontal axle carrying a paddle wheel below a simple channel or trough edge. Joints: the wheel rotates continuously about the axle axis. Use a taller support so the rotating stage sits high above the base.

A truck-bed style missile launcher module without the vehicle. A rectangular base frame carries a slewing ring and an elevation arm that supports a bank of cylindrical launch tubes. A stainless steel juicer with a deep base, a clear upper chamber, and a tall feed pusher centered over the basket housing. prompt: A small sewing box with a plain top lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Favor thick side walls and a heavy frame around the moving panel.
A children's three-drawer dresser with a rectangular carcass and three wide drawers. Each drawer slides prismatically on wooden bottom-edge guide rails.

A clip-on drafting lamp with a double-jaw clamp base, a two-link arm, and a cone shade. The first link revolves on a revolute joint at the clamp post top. The second link pivots on a revolute elbow. The shade tilts on a revolute joint at the tip.

A single-sash sliding window. A rectangular outer frame contains one fixed frame segment and one sliding sash. Joints: the sash translates prismatically along horizontal guide channels. Use a tall narrow frame around the sliding member.

A fork-supported roll cartridge. A grounded fork frame supports a short cylindrical roll cartridge centered between the fork arms. The cartridge rotates on one revolute joint about its longitudinal supported axis.
A strap-and-leaf hatch hinge. A grounded back leaf supports a longer strap-like moving leaf through spaced knuckles and a straight pin. The strap leaf is one revolute joint about the hinge pin axis.

A swivel bar stool with a trumpet base, a ring footrest, a low back, and a side-mounted knob for backrest tension. The seat rotates continuously about the vertical pedestal axis, the backrest tilts on a short rear hinge, and the side knob rotates continuously on its short shaft. Lounge bar scale.

A bridge-tied dual-column lift. Two grounded columns are tied by a lower foot and upper bridge, and a front carriage spans between them with visible guide sleeves. The front carriage is one vertical prismatic joint on the fixed columns.

A microscope-style XY translation table. A broad fixed base supports a long lower carriage and a narrower upper saddle turned orthogonal to it, ending at a perforated or plain top plate. The lower carriage is one horizontal prismatic joint and the upper saddle is a second horizontal prismatic joint at right angles.

A wall-backed lift mast with forks. A rigid back frame carries vertical guides and a front-moving carriage plate with twin fork tines and a short load guard. The front plate and forks move together as one prismatic joint on the mast.

A inspection shutter array with separate vanes. A fixed outer frame carries a bank of parallel rigid vanes with simple rectangular cross sections and visible pivot pins at the ends. Each vane rotates on an independent revolute joint about its long axis.

A screw-in light bulb seated in a socket. A bulb envelope sits above a threaded metal base, and the socket body holds the mating threaded collar. Joints: the bulb rotates continuously about the shared socket axis. Favor thick collars, shoulders, or end caps around the axis.

A record turntable. A plinth supports a central platter and a tonearm base off to one side. Joints: the platter rotates continuously about vertical, and the tonearm swings on a revolute pivot at the tonearm base. Favor large bearing housings and thick side supports around the rotary stage.

A pedestal-mounted coaxial rotary fixture. A grounded foot supports a vertical spindle with multiple coaxial carriers stepped upward from largest to smallest. Each carrier is a separate revolute joint about the common spindle axis.

A three-stage telescoping slide. Three nested rectangular members extend from a fixed outer sleeve to a small end plate. The middle and inner members are serial prismatic joints along the same axis with about 120 mm of travel each.

A bench module with a base rail, a sliding carriage, a shoulder block on the carriage, and a two-link arm ending in a pad. The carriage should be long enough to read as its own positioning stage before the arm begins. The carriage is a prismatic joint along the base, the shoulder is a revolute joint on the carriage, and the elbow is a second revolute joint farther out on the arm.

A inspection gantry with center truck. Two base guides support a traveling crossbeam with visible end supports, and the crossbeam carries a compact center truck. The crossbeam is one prismatic joint along the base, and the center truck is a second prismatic joint across the crossbeam.

A skateboard with standard trucks. A deck supports front and rear truck hangers, each holding one axle and two wheels. Joints: each wheel spins continuously about its axle, and each truck steers on a revolute kingpin axis. Favor thick forks, stout axle blocks, and chunky frame sections.

A benchtop positioning stage with a long X base, a crosswise Y carriage on top of it, and a vertical Z slide ending in a square tool plate. The three guide directions should be visually obvious and not collapse into one box. The X, Y, and Z motions are stacked orthogonal prismatic joints.

An under-slung twin-jaw gripper. A top support carries a compact center body and two hanging jaw carriages that close beneath it. The two hanging carriages are independent prismatic joints on the same axis.

category_title: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector
prompt: An inspection rail module with a low base guide, a sliding carriage block, and a compact vertical spindle nose mounted on the carriage. The carriage and spindle should read as separate mechanical subsystems with visibly different support geometry and sensible clearance between them. Give it real machine detail with bearing blocks, machined rail pads, spindle caps, cover strips, and enough carriage mass that the rotary head feels grounded. The carriage slides prismatically along the rail axis, and the spindle rotates as its own revolute joint about a vertical local axis. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.
A compact service two-stage axis. A broad grounded body supports one main carriage and one smaller terminal carriage. The two stages are serial prismatic joints.

A hinged bollard barrier with a round steel bollard post that folds to horizontal. The post folds on a revolute hinge at its base plate about a horizontal axis. A padlock receiver is fixed to the post base.

A hinged service access panel on an equipment face. A rectangular frame surrounds the opening, and a panel door hangs from one side with a latch edge opposite. Joints: the door is a single revolute panel about a vertical side hinge axis. Use a taller, narrower body with a deep frame around the hinge line.

A bedside arc reading lamp with a weighted square base, a long boom arm, and a small adjustable shade at the end. The boom arm pivots on a revolute hinge at the base post top. The shade tilts on a revolute hinge at the arm end bracket.

A tower-mounted pitch-axis trunnion module. A short pedestal rises from a heavy foot and ends in a compact axis package whose moving member is visually smaller than the support. One revolute joint tilts the carried face about a horizontal trunnion axis supported by side cheeks.

An offset indexing head with a low rotary base, a raised side support arm, and a smaller spin head mounted off to one side on a parallel upright axis. The base rotates about the main vertical axis, and the upper head rotates about a second vertical axis offset from the first.

A box-section two-stage telescoping slide. A grounded outer section carries one nested inner runner with a plain front face and clear overlap. The inner runner is one prismatic joint along the slide axis.

An attic hatch folding loft ladder with a ceiling-mounted frame and three hinged stile sections. The uppermost section attaches to the hatch frame on a revolute hinge at the frame edge. Each lower section folds at the knee on a revolute hinge.

Design a realistic, highly detailed prismatic-revolute chain as a standalone mechanical study assembly for the sdk_hybrid pipeline, with a linear slide feeding a rotary joint at the distal end. Keep it as a pure mechanical assembly with explicit joints, guides, bearings, brackets, and support structure. Avoid product styling, decorative housings, or recognizable household or device forms.

A modern steel campanile bell tower with a square steel lattice shaft, a flat cap at the top, and a large bell hung on a beam inside the open belfry stage. The bell hangs on a revolute hinge at the headstock across two lattice columns. The bell swings about the horizontal axis.

category_title: Roof vent tower with hinged flap

A heavy-duty anti-vehicle bollard with a thick cylindrical ram, a reinforced ground socket, and a broad crown plate carrying a keyway. A two-axis gimbal module. A yaw turntable base carries a U-shaped fork, and the fork supports a pitch cradle with a small faceplate between its arms. Yaw is a revolute joint about the vertical base axis, and pitch is a revolute joint about the horizontal fork axis.

A hospital stretcher elevator with a wide rectangular car and two wide sliding door panels. The car slides prismatically on vertical guide rail columns. Each door panel slides prismatically sideways on its guide rail track.

A manual wheelchair. A chair frame supports two large rear wheels, two front casters, a seat, a backrest, and footrests. A three-carriage slide chain. A grounded outer guide carries a middle stage, the middle stage carries an inner stage, and the inner stage carries a short tip stage. The mechanism uses three serial prismatic joints.

A three-arm turnstile gate. A central column supports a rotor hub with three radial arms inside a fixed frame. Joints: the arm hub rotates continuously about the vertical center axis. Favor large bearing housings and thick side supports around the rotary stage.

A wheelbarrow. A tray sits between two handles, with one front wheel in a fork and two resting legs at the rear. Joints: the front wheel rotates continuously about its axle. Keep the moving wheels small relative to a larger body or tray.

A low-profile three-joint revolute chain. A broad mounting plate anchors a flatter set of links with offset knuckles and a compact distal a compact end tab. All three joints are revolute joints bending in one plane from the grounded root.

An outdoor GRP weatherproof enclosure on a pole with a wide rectangular body and a hinged lid. The lid hinges on two revolute knuckles at the lid's rear edge. A compression latch on the lid front operates on a revolute cam-over joint.

A stadium four-head floodlight mast with a round pole, a top cross-arm, and four individual lamp heads. The cross-arm is fixed to the pole top. Each lamp head tilts on its own revolute tilt joint at the arm mount bracket.

A low-profile vertical slide carrying a hinged bracket. A broad lower mount supports a vertical guide stage and a small forward-projecting hinged face on the moving block. The moving block is a vertical prismatic joint and the bracket is a revolute joint carried by that block.

A ladder-frame four-joint revolute chain. A grounded root bracket carries five rigid links in series with long side plates and a compact end tab at the tip. The chain uses four serial revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane.

A side-mounted cantilever arm. A stiff backplate or post supports two serial links and a compact wrist face, keeping the root support clearly off to one side of the moving chain. Three revolute joints create shoulder, elbow, and wrist motion on supported axes.

A finger-like phalanx chain. A grounded knuckle block carries three tapered phalanx links with clear side cheeks and pin joints, ending in a blunt fingertip pad. The chain has three serial revolute joints bending in one plane.

A vacuum cleaner with an articulated wand. A main body connects to a rigid wand chain ending in a floor nozzle. Joints: the wand bends on one or two revolute elbows, and the floor nozzle pitches on a horizontal revolute hinge. Use fewer, larger structural pieces with broad simple profiles.

A teaching-studio drafting table with a flat H-base, a vertical carriage rail, and a medium rectangular board with a bottom ledge. A single-leaf drawbridge. A fixed shore frame carries one bridge leaf on heavy side bearings. Joints: the bridge leaf is a single revolute panel about a horizontal shore-side axis. Use a low, wide body with the moving panel spanning most of the width.

A compact operator chair with a simple seat, a backrest hinge, and a small under-seat lever. A service panel axis with a hanging slide. A broad fixed back frame carries a moving horizontal crosshead, and the crosshead supports a narrow Z slide tucked below it. The crosshead and the lower slide are prismatic joints on orthogonal axes.
An under-slung hinge-slide unit. A top support carries a hanging arm and a compact slider at the arm tip. The hanging arm is a revolute joint and the tip slider is a prismatic joint.

A bridge-mounted folding arm chain. A fixed bridge support carries a root clevis and a sequence of links whose section sizes taper toward the distal small end pad. The chain has three revolute joints in series.

An under-slung trunnion positioner. A top support carries a lower rotary stage and a hanging work table held on trunnion supports. The lower stage is a revolute joint and the hanging table is a second revolute joint on the trunnion axis.

A screw-cap bottle. A bottle body rises to a threaded neck, capped by a separate screw closure. Joints: the cap rotates continuously about the bottle neck axis. Use a short, wide proportion with the rotating element dominating the front face.

A fidget cube with four side-face toggle levers each on a revolute hinge pivot at the lever base edge, a click-ball roller set in a socket on a revolute joint on the top face, and a smooth spinning dial on a revolute joint on the bottom face.

A compact laptop-style overbed table with a narrow offset base, a slender telescoping column, a rectangular top, and a front retaining lip, plus locking casters at the base corners. the support head keeps the top centered over the column rather than cantilevering from a hidden arm, and the wheel forks remain individually visible under the base frame. A countertop toaster convection oven with a wide rectangular housing, a front drop-down door, and a pull-out crumb tray. The door drops on a revolute hinge at the door's bottom horizontal edge. The crumb tray slides prismatically out from under the front opening.

A classic wall thermostat with a large central dial and a short bottom slider for mode selection. The dial rotates continuously about the center axis, and the bottom slider translates prismatically along its short guide slot. Wall thermostat scale.

An open reduction gearbox with parallel shafts. A grounded housing base exposes several parallel shafts with one obvious gear on each stage and no enclosing lid. Each shaft is modeled as its own revolute joint.

A school entrance three-wing revolving door with a gentle-speed brake mechanism. A central post carries three equal rectangular panel wings to the drum wall. The wing assembly rotates continuously about the vertical post axis.

A slide-and-lift XZ stage. A grounded horizontal base slide carries a moving carriage, and the carriage carries a compact vertical guide with a top pad. The lower X stage and upper Z stage are orthogonal prismatic joints.

Giant astronomical observation binoculars on a parallelogram fork mount. Two large cylindrical objective tubes are fixed to a wide bridge cradle. The cradle rides on a revolute altitude joint in the fork. The fork pivots on a continuous azimuth bearing on a pillar.

A standalone all-in-one DJ controller with a wide rectangular housing, two jog wheels, a mixer section with channel faders, and two deck sections. Each jog wheel rotates continuously. Each fader slides prismatically in its vertical slot.

A wireless electronic dropper seatpost with a round outer tube, a smooth inner actuator post on a prismatic joint, a battery compartment sealed by a screw-cap on a revolute hinge at the tube side, a single-bolt saddle rail clamp plate at the inner post crown, and a saddle with a molded composite shell and two steel rails clamped into the plate on a tilt-adjust revolute pivot.

A front stub-axle kingpin assembly with a rectangular beam axle, two stub spindles on kingpin pivots, and wheel hubs. Each stub spindle revolves on a revolute kingpin joint at the beam end. Each wheel hub rotates continuously on the spindle bearing.

A barn ventilation fan with a large square housing and four hinged outer shutter flap panels. Each shutter panel pivots on a revolute hinge at the panel's outer edge. The fan blade inside rotates continuously. A speed dial knob on the housing rotates continuously.

A wall-mounted mailbox with a front access door and a small top rain cap. The box body is fixed to the wall, the front door is hinged along one side, and a rotary latch sits at the opposite edge. The front door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the latch rotates on its own short pivot.

A stacking support fold-out arm. A grounded side plate carries four short links that can nest close together and end in a plain platform face. The links are connected by four serial revolute joints in one plane.

A yaw-shoulder wristed arm. A grounded base pedestal supports an upper arm, a forearm, and a short wrist link ending in a tool flange. The shoulder, elbow, and wrist are three serial revolute joints with the wrist carried at the tip of the forearm.

A full-size speedlight with a deep battery housing, a tall neck, and a wide flash head with rounded side cheeks, plus a rear control panel with several raised buttons around a small screen. the head cradle keeps the support geometry exposed instead of hiding it inside the shell, and the button bank remains clearly articulated in the back shell. Four rotary knobs occupy the center of the control rail, with one shorter knob on each far end and two push-buttons tucked directly under the middle pair. The six knobs rotate continuously about front-to-back revolute axes, the buttons are short prismatic plungers, and the oven door rotates downward on a bottom-edge hinge axis.

A full-tower workstation with a tall rectangular steel body, a large side panel on two rear revolute hinge pins, a front door panel on a left-edge revolute hinge covering drive bays, and a removable top ventilation mesh lid on two rear revolute hinge pins.

A pair of safety glasses with broad side shields and extendable temple arms. The front lens frame carries two hinged temples, and each temple has a telescoping rear section for fit adjustment. Each temple folds on a revolute hinge at the outer corner, and each rear temple segment slides prismatically along the temple axis within its sleeve. Keep each telescoping rear temple section clipped inside its sleeve so the arm extends without the rear segment pulling free.

A side-wall slide with wristed extension. A grounded side plate supports a slide carriage, the carriage supports a hinged body, and the hinged body supports a small linear extension. The articulation order is prismatic, revolute, then prismatic.

An under-slung joystick assembly. A top support bracket carries an outer yoke and an inner yoke below it, with the stick projecting downward from the innermost member. Two orthogonal revolute joints support the stick through the nested yokes.

Model Windshield sun visor as an industrial safety-first variant for heavy duty cycles. Start from a visor panel on a roof hinge arm plus secondary swing pivot, then add guards, lockouts, over-travel stops, and reinforcement at high-stress joints. Favor visible fastener logic and plate/brace load paths over decorative surfacing. Primary and secondary pivots should be visible and mechanically credible. All subparts must remain connected to a parent support; no unsupported fragments. No floating parts.

Build a precision professional Searchlight tower tuned for calibration and repeatable alignment. Use a mast/tower support carrying a spotlight head in a pan-tilt yoke with datum-friendly surfaces, index marks, adjustment features, and controlled gap language. Visual style can be minimal, but kinematics must be explicit. Pan and tilt axes should be separated and carried by yoke/bearing blocks. Keep a fully connected mechanical stack with no free-floating parts. No floating parts.

A wide bench playground swing with side suspension arms. The bench seat hangs from two rigid side arms connected to the playground crossbeam above. The bench rotates on the upper hanger pivots at the crossbeam and on the lower pivots at the seat sides.

A regional-airliner turbofan with a broad intake lip and a long smooth nacelle. The engine has a central spinner, a large front fan, and a short exhaust cone aligned within the rear of the nacelle. The front fan rotor rotates continuously about the longitudinal center axis. Keep the front fan rotor clipped to the center hub so the fan stays seated within the nacelle during rotation.

A large industrial three-blade HVLS ceiling fan with a round hub and three wide rectangular paddle blades on long arms. The blade and hub assembly rotates continuously about the vertical axis. Each blade arm is fixed to the hub.

A low-profile benchtop monocular microscope with a thick base, a short curved arm, an angled eyepiece tube, and a compact square stage. The head carriage slides prismatically along a vertical guide on the arm, the stage carrier slides prismatically left to right beneath the stage plate, and the objective nosepiece rotates on the central turret joint.

A laboratory homogenizer blender with a tall rectangular housing, a locking stainless chamber cup, and a blade drive. The chamber cup locks on a revolute bayonet twist. A hinged chamber lid opens on a revolute hinge at the chamber rear edge. The blade rotates continuously.

A mill waterwheel with a broad rim, deep buckets, and a side brake shoe lever. The wheel is carried on a horizontal axle between sturdy support frames, and a brake arm at one side can press a shoe against the rim. The waterwheel rotates continuously about its axle, and the brake lever rotates on its own pivot near the support frame. Keep the brake shoe lever clipped to its support pivot so the arm stays attached while swinging toward the rim.

A hand syringe. A cylindrical barrel holds a plunger rod and plunger head, ending in a nozzle tip. Joints: the plunger translates prismatically along the barrel axis. Add one fixed stop block or end frame that clearly limits travel.

Design a retrofit/legacy-inspired Wind turbine that blends old-school construction cues with modern reliability. Keep the core as a tower with nacelle, rotor hub, and blade-root interfaces; include service hatches, bolted adapters, and pragmatic reinforcements where components meet. Rotor and blade roots should be hub-mounted with realistic load paths. The final model must be one connected mechanism where every element is structurally supported. No floating parts.

An executive floor globe with a large sphere above a tripod stand and a circular map shelf. The sphere sits in a partial meridian on polar pivots, and the support frame rises from the tripod center through a simple rotating collar above the shelf. The sphere rotates about its polar axis, and the upper support turns about a vertical axis at the collar so the globe can be reoriented over the stand. Keep the upper globe support clipped into the rotating collar above the shelf so the globe assembly stays seated over the stand.

A compact capsule-and-candy vending machine with a clear hopper, a rectangular lower body, and a front service panel beneath the dispensing wheel. The dispensing wheel rotates continuously about its horizontal axle, the front service panel rotates on a vertical side hinge, and the retrieval flap rotates on a small horizontal hinge below the chute.

An A3 wide-format flatbed scanner with a wide rectangular body, a lid on two barrel revolute hinges spanning the full rear width, and a retractable document guide strip sliding on a prismatic joint along the lid inner face for media alignment.

A cargo folding scooter with a flat deck carrying a front cargo basket, two deck wheels on revolute axles, a folding handlebar stem on a revolute hinge at the deck front neck, and a rear kickstand prop on a revolute hinge at the deck rear underside.

A corner-cabinet lazy susan with two kidney-shaped shelves on a vertical pole. The top and bottom shelves are attached to a central shaft inside the cabinet corner, leaving the cutout sides toward the door opening. The shelf assembly rotates continuously about the vertical pole axis. Keep the kidney-shaped shelves clipped to the vertical center shaft so the shelf assembly stays seated while turning.

A long-reach TV wall arm without the display. A wall plate carries a first link, a second link, and a rectangular VESA-style head with a short tilt yoke. Two revolute joints fold the links from the wall, and the head uses one swivel revolute and one tilt revolute.

category_title: Laptop stand with articulated height adjustment
prompt: A desktop laptop stand with a flat base plate, a rear vertical mast, and a single rigid top tray carried on a sliding carriage near the mast head. The tray has two front retaining lips and remains one rigid supported body above the carriage. The carriage slides prismatically along the vertical mast axis, and the tray rotates on a horizontal tilt hinge at the carriage head.

A 2x teleconverter with two cylindrical bayonet mount halves joined by a short cylindrical body. The front bayonet interface ring rotates on a revolute joint for mounting. The rear bayonet interface ring is fixed. An alignment peg ring revolves on a revolute joint.

Design a retrofit/legacy-inspired Usb drive with swivel cover that blends old-school construction cues with modern reliability. Keep the core as a USB drive body with connector and side-pinned swivel cover; include service hatches, bolted adapters, and pragmatic reinforcements where components meet. Swivel cover movement should be constrained by a visible pivot pin. The final model must be one connected mechanism where every element is structurally supported. No floating parts.

A stick vacuum with a folding wand. A motor body connects to a straight wand and a floor head, with the fold joint placed between the body and the wand. Joints: the fold joint is revolute about a horizontal axis, and the floor head pitches on a second revolute hinge. Offset the moving chain to one side of the fixed support.

A Maksutov catadioptric telescope on a single-arm computerized alt-az mount. A short fat Maksutov tube body clamps in a saddle on a revolute altitude axis at the arm top. The arm rotates continuously on an azimuth bearing at the tripod head.

A slide-out visor range hood with a fixed upper body, a telescoping front visor, and a small side rocker plus one rotary knob. The front visor slides prismatically forward on straight internal guides, the rocker pivots on a short local hinge, and the knob rotates continuously on its side-facing shaft. Compact apartment kitchen scale.

A compound cross-slide drill table with a fixed base, a column, a drill head, and a two-axis XY table. The lower table slides prismatically along the Y-axis. The upper table slides prismatically along the X-axis on top. The quill drops prismatically inside the head.

A compact home-video camcorder with a tapered body, a front objective barrel, and a wide side display door. The display door rotates outward on a vertical hinge along the left body side, the eyepiece diopter wheel rotates on a small transverse shaft at the rear viewfinder, and the focus ring rotates continuously around the lens barrel. Consumer product scale with believable handheld proportions.

A simple rice cooker with a cylindrical body, a domed upper lid, and a small front control panel, plus a single cook-warm selector dial on the front. the body has a slightly inset lower band and a clear front latch zone, and the dial sits below the latch rather than floating in the shell. The lid rotates on a rear hinge at the rim, and the selector dial rotates continuously about its central axle. Small household appliance scale.

category_title: Pedal trash bin with hinged lid
prompt: A designer step bin with a curved front body and a split top lid. The body is fixed, and the two lid flaps are mounted along the rear edge beside a single front pedal. The two lid flaps rotate on separate rear hinge axes, and the pedal rotates on its own transverse pivot at the lower front.

A service-tool wrist with yaw, pitch, and roll. A grounded base bracket supports a yaw cartridge, the yaw cartridge supports a small pitch frame, and the pitch frame supports a cylindrical roll output. The articulation sequence is yaw revolute, pitch revolute, and roll revolute with each axis physically supported by the preceding structure.

A recumbent trike front fork with a wide squared-crown blade pair, two straight round blade legs, and a threaded steerer. A forward-swept bullhorn handlebar pair clamps at the stem extension tip pinch collar; the full steering assembly pivots on a nearly vertical revolute head-tube axis.

A folding A-frame step ladder. A front climbing frame and a rear support frame meet at a top cap, with wide treads on the front frame. Joints: the rear frame folds relative to the front frame on a single revolute hinge at the top cap. Tuck the hinge hardware into the top cap for a cleaner silhouette.

Create a cost-optimized mass-manufacturable Robotic leg. Base architecture: a hip-knee-ankle chain with load-bearing limb shells and actuator bays. Reduce part count, simplify molds/extrusions/stampings, and keep assembly order obvious with realistic snap/bolt/clamp interfaces. Show leg articulation as true serial kinematics with plausible range limits. Every piece must have a believable attachment path; no floating or orphaned components. No floating parts.

A slant-top casino machine with an angled screen panel, a button deck, and a lockable belly door beneath the play area. The belly door rotates on a vertical side revolute hinge, a spin button on the deck translates prismatically in its sleeve, and a volume knob near the screen rotates continuously about its shaft.

A flat wall-panel air purifier with a thin rectangular housing mounted flush to a wall and a front-access hinged filter panel. The filter panel swings on a revolute hinge at the panel's left vertical edge. An inner filter cartridge slides prismatically out on bottom guide rails.

A retractable utility knife with a main blade slider on top and a hinged spare-blade compartment door in the handle side. The handle shell contains the blade carrier in a straight channel and a small storage cavity behind a side cover. The blade carrier slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the storage door rotates on a small side hinge in the handle shell.

A molded equipment case with a deep lower shell and a shallow lid on a full-width rear hinge. The case has front draw latches and a center carry handle on the closing edge. The lid swings on the rear revolute hinge axis, and each draw latch rotates on its local pivot to clamp the lid shut.

A road double crankset with a tapered spindle, two crank arms, a large outer chainring, and a smaller inner chainring both fixed to the right crank spider. The spindle and crank arms rotate continuously. Each pedal rotates continuously.

A tabletop portal gantry with two side uprights, a spanning top beam, a carriage traveling along that beam, and a Z slide hanging from the carriage. The frame should feel like a real automation bridge with clear guide paths on the beam and on the hanging slide. Make it read like production machine hardware with bolted rail mounts, stiffened legs, carriage covers, and believable end-stop geometry on both axes. The carriage is a horizontal prismatic axis along the beam, and the hanging ram is a vertical prismatic axis on the carriage. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A wall-tunnel pet door with an outer flap, an inner flap, and a selector dial on the trim frame. The two flaps are mounted at opposite ends of the tunnel on separate top hinges, and the dial sits on the inner frame face. Each flap rotates on its own horizontal upper hinge axis, and the selector dial rotates on its local axis in the frame. Keep both flaps clipped to their separate top hinge lines inside the tunnel so each panel stays attached while swinging.

An all-terrain knee scooter with a broad knee pad, a steerable front fork, and large pneumatic wheels. The rectangular main frame carries two rear wheels on fixed supports, while the front pair is mounted in a steerable fork assembly below the handlebar column. The front fork yaws about the steering axis, and all four wheels spin continuously on their axle supports. Keep the front wheel pair clipped into the steerable fork so the front assembly stays captured during steering.

A side-hinged casement window in a rectangular frame. A glazed sash mounts to one jamb on two hinges and carries a crank or lever handle at the opposite side. The sash rotates on a vertical revolute hinge axis at the jamb, and the handle rotates on its own small pivot.

A pivoting dock board with a flat ribbed-steel deck, a rear cleated edge on a revolute hinge pin at the dock face frame so the deck rocks to bridge the gap between dock and truck bed, and two folding side safety curbs each on a revolute hinge at the deck long-edge mounts.

A broadcast fader controller with a flat rectangular housing carrying four motorized linear faders and a row of endless encoder knobs. Each fader carriage slides prismatically in its slot. Each encoder knob spins continuously about a vertical axis.

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A compact two-branch inspection tree. A boxed spine supports two side-mounted rotary branches on separate brackets, with the branch lengths and mounting heights intentionally varied so the silhouette does not collapse into symmetry. The two branches are independent revolute joints on their own supported axes.

A boxed industrial lever chain. A reinforced base cheek supports deeper-section links with visible pin bosses, and the final link terminates in a small end tab. The assembly is a serial chain of three revolute joints about parallel hinge axes.

A shoulder-elbow-extension chain. A grounded base bracket supports two hinged links, and the distal link supports a short slider at its tip. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints, and the tip stage is a prismatic joint.

An appliance hand truck with a tall frame, strap hooks, and a folding nose plate. The main frame carries two large rear wheels, and the broad lower plate is hinged so it can fold flat against the uprights for storage. The wheels spin on their axle, and the nose plate rotates on a transverse revolute hinge at the frame base.

A washer with a drum-view transparent door — a white rectangular body, a drum on a continuous revolute axle, a wide-window porthole door on the left edge on two barrel revolute hinges, and a soap dispenser drawer on a prismatic joint at the body top left face.

A prep food processor with a shallow wide bowl, a broad feed opening, two nested pushers, and a compact front interface with two rocker switches beside a selector knob. The bowl twists on a short continuous lock at the base coupling, the large pusher and small inner pusher slide prismatically in the feed opening, and the slicing disc rotates continuously on the center shaft. The selector knob rotates continuously, and both rocker switches pivot independently on short axes. The bowl and feed assembly should stay visibly hollow with a believable working cavity. Low wide countertop scale.

A surveillance blimp with a long envelope, a small sensor gondola, and side-mounted engine nacelles aft of the cabin, plus vectoring engine pylons beside the gondola. the tail assembly remains visibly separate with distinct vertical and horizontal control surfaces, and the engine pods stay visibly carried by those pylons. The engine propellers spin continuously on their local axes, the rudder rotates on its vertical hinge, and the elevators rotate on horizontal tail hinges, and the engine pods rotate on short pivots at the pylon ends. Observation airship scale.

A workshop tote-on-wheels with a rectangular lower body, a hinged top compartment lid, and a telescoping handle with a wide cross grip. The handle stages slide prismatically on parallel rails, the top compartment lid rotates on a rear hinge, and the rear wheel pair rotates continuously on the transport axle. Medium-duty maintenance-cart scale.

A desk fan with a tilting head. A base supports a guarded fan head between two side brackets. Joints: the fan rotor spins continuously about its axle, and the head tilts on a horizontal revolute axis through the side brackets. Use fewer, larger fixed support pieces with broad simple sections.

A remote weapon station with a missile pod on one side and an optic turret on the other. The upper structure sits on a rotating pedestal, the central weapon cradle elevates between side supports, and the optic pod is mounted on a small pan-tilt bracket beside the weapon. The pedestal rotates about a vertical azimuth axis, the weapon cradle rotates about a horizontal elevation axis, and the optic pod rotates on its small tilt axis at the end of its support bracket. Keep the optic pod clipped to its support bracket so the sensor assembly stays attached while tilting beside the weapon cradle.

A modern countertop stand mixer with a low wide base, a deep brushed-steel bowl on a small carriage, and a streamlined head carrying a dough hook. The bowl must move prismatically relative to the base on a short fore-aft adjustment slide, the head must tilt on one rear horizontal revolute hinge, the tool shaft must rotate continuously about the vertical mixing axis, the speed selector on the base must rotate on a small revolute joint, and the head-lock control on the base must translate prismatically.

A side-swing pickup tailgate with a spare-tire cutout and a latch lever. The gate panel is mounted to one rear bed corner on vertical hinges and closes against the opposite corner with a lever latch. The tailgate rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the lever latch rotates on its own short pivot near the meeting edge.

A French sketch-box easel with two front legs and a single rear leg forming an A-frame, plus a storage box panel below the canvas rest. The rear leg extends prismatically from the top apex for height. The front legs spread on a revolute hinge at the apex. The canvas tray slides prismatically on the front leg.

A compact wrist pitch-roll head. A grounded pitch yoke carries a short roll cartridge ending in a simple output flange. The module uses one pitch revolute joint about the side trunnions and one roll revolute joint about the flange axis.

An undershot waterwheel. Two side frames support a horizontal axle carrying a paddle wheel below a simple channel or trough edge. Joints: the wheel rotates continuously about the axle axis. Add one fixed guard hoop, ring, or secondary frame around the rotating stage.

A pedestal-mounted missile launcher with a boxy launch pod. A fixed base supports a yaw turntable, which carries a tilting cradle with the rectangular canister pack mounted on top. The launcher yaws about a vertical revolute axis at the base and pitches on a horizontal revolute axis at the cradle sides.

A stainless steel juicer with a broad motor base, a transparent hinged lid, and a cylindrical plunger that lifts vertically from the feed tube at the center of the lid. A selector dial and a small pulse button are mounted on the front of the housing, and a spinning grater basket is visible through the clear upper chamber. The plunger slides prismatically along the feed tube axis, the lid rotates on a rear hinge, the selector dial rotates on its front axis, the pulse button moves on a short prismatic plunger, and the grater basket rotates continuously on the vertical drive axis.

A floor-standing pendulum metronome with a tall narrow rectangular housing on a flat base. A long pendulum rod extends well above the housing top, carrying a large cylindrical adjustable weight near the upper end; the rod pivots from a shaft near the housing base interior. Pendulum swings on a revolute at the internal pivot shaft; weight slides prismatically along the rod; winding key on one side face rotates continuously.

A desktop Wi-Fi router with four external antennas. A low rectangular housing carries four antenna stalks on hinge bases along the rear edge, and a small reset or pairing button is recessed into the body. Each antenna tilts on its own revolute root hinge, and the button moves as a short prismatic plunger into the housing.

A traditional windmill with a rotating sail hub. A tower body supports a cap and a front hub carrying multiple lattice blades. Joints: the blade hub rotates continuously about the windmill shaft axis, and the cap rotates about the vertical tower axis. Offset the rotating member from the main support column or base center.

A bench monitor arm with a long reach. A heavy clamp or post base supports two articulated links ending in a VESA plate with a small tilt hinge behind it. The mount uses two arm-fold revolute joints, one head swivel revolute, and one head tilt revolute.

A compact party speaker with a recessed top handle, a rotary volume knob, and a row of front buttons above the main grille, plus a power rocker and two menu buttons. the enclosure is tall and portable rather than a fixed home speaker cabinet, and the rocker stays distinct from the two smaller buttons beside it. The handle rotates upward on two side hinges and the volume knob rotates continuously on its central axis, and the power rocker rotates on a short pivot and the two menu buttons each depress independently. Portable party-speaker scale.

A double-barrel floor pump with one shared T-handle and twin cylinders. The broad base supports two parallel pump barrels, and the single T-handle bridges the tops of both sliding rods. The T-handle and the two linked rods translate prismatically along the parallel cylinder axes.

category_title: Dishwasher with dropdown door and sliding racks
prompt: A European-style built-in dishwasher with a flat front, a dropdown door, two deep sliding racks, a narrow top cutlery tray, and a concealed top-edge control strip. The door rotates downward on a bottom horizontal revolute hinge, the upper rack, lower rack, and cutlery tray each slide prismatically on their own guides, and the upper and lower spray arms rotate continuously on central hubs inside the tub. A fold-down stemware shelf on the upper rack rotates on matched side pivots, the detergent-cup cover on the inner door opens on a small hinge, and each visible top-edge button is a separate small prismatic push button. The wash tub should read as a hollow thin-wall cavity with open rack volumes rather than a solid block. Built-in kitchen scale.

Generate a compact apartment/desktop version of Robotic arms. Keep footprint and envelope efficient while preserving full functional articulation from a base pedestal feeding a shoulder-elbow-wrist chain with clear joint cartridges. Emphasize fold-flat or stow-friendly behavior where relevant, plus safe clearances around moving parts. Make axis order and joint offsets readable so motion paths are unambiguous. Ensure a single coherent assembly graph with zero floaters. No floating parts.

A stationary exercise bike. A floor frame supports the flywheel housing, crank set, seat post, and handlebar mast. Joints: the crank rotates continuously about the bottom-bracket axis, the flywheel rotates continuously about its axle, and the seat post adjusts prismatically along its column. Use fewer, larger frame pieces with long uninterrupted sections.

A cinema quadcopter with a rectangular body, four folding arms, and a three-axis camera gimbal under the nose. Each arm folds on a revolute hinge at the body. The gimbal has a tilt revolute joint and a roll revolute joint stacked. Each propeller rotates continuously.

A woodworking front vise with a wide flat fixed jaw bolted to a bench front, a wide moving jaw face, and a threaded lead-screw with a round handle. The moving jaw slides prismatically toward and away from the fixed jaw along horizontal guide rods. The lead-screw handle rotates continuously about the screw axis.

A rigid kick scooter with a long deck, a T-bar handle, and a rear fender brake above the back wheel. The front wheel is carried by a fork and steering column, while the rear wheel sits at the tail under the springy brake fender. The wheels spin on their axles, the front fork yaws on a vertical steering revolute axis, and the brake fender rotates downward on a hinge at the rear deck.

category_title: Top-load washing machine with hinged lid
prompt: A premium top-load washer with a glass-window lid and a broad rear console with a central selector dial flanked by six program buttons. The lid rotates upward on a rear hinge, the wash tub rotates continuously about the vertical axis, and the selector dial rotates continuously on the console. All six program buttons are separate prismatic push controls, and the front lid-release button below the handle slides prismatically into the top deck. The tub and lid opening must read as a true hollow laundry chamber with visible depth and inner wall thickness. Full-size laundry-room scale.

category_title: Desktop pencil sharpener with hand crank and drawer
prompt: A premium desktop pencil sharpener with a rectangular body, a large side crank, a front drawer with a finger pull, and a circular point-adjustment dial around the pencil port. The pencil opening reads as a real hollow channel leading into the cutter cavity and the dial stays separate from the front shell. The crank rotates continuously on its side hub, the front drawer slides prismatically along the body depth axis, and the point-adjustment dial rotates continuously around the pencil-entry axis. Premium desk-accessory scale.

category_title: Adjustable weight bench with hinged backrest
prompt: An adjustable decline bench with a long back pad, an articulated seat, and a front leg-roller assembly, plus a front carry handle between the wheels. the frame has a low wheel set at one end for moving the bench around the room, and the handle projects from the front crossmember without floating. The back pad rotates on a central horizontal hinge, the seat rotates on its own hinge ahead of it, the paired leg rollers spin continuously on their support axle, and the transport wheels rotate continuously on the frame axle. Full home-workout bench scale.

A pair of over-ear headphones with a padded headband and two circular earcups. Each earcup is carried by a short yoke at the end of a headband arm, and each side includes a fold hinge above the cup mount. Each cup swivels on its yoke revolute axis for fit adjustment, and each side assembly folds inward on a second revolute hinge between the arm and yoke.

A passenger-side glove box with a curved outer door panel and inner storage tub. The tub is fixed inside a dashboard face, and the door is supported by side hinge arms near the lower corners. The door opens on a lower-edge revolute axis with about 70 degrees of travel.

A ventless condensation dryer with a slim white body, a drum on a revolute axle, a circular porthole door with a chrome bar handle hinged on the left edge on revolute hinges, and a lint filter drawer at the base sliding on a prismatic joint.

A ring light on a boom stand with a yawing boom arm and a small counterweight. The vertical stand rises from a tripod base, the boom arm rotates from the stand top, and the ring light tilts at the boom tip on a compact yoke. The boom arm rotates about a vertical yaw axis at the stand top, the ring head rotates about a horizontal tilt axis at the boom tip, the center mast slides prismatically for height adjustment, and the tripod legs rotate on their hinge pivots at the base. Keep the boom arm clipped to its yaw joint at the stand top so the arm stays attached while rotating around the column.

A toy race car with a low wedge body, a central cockpit canopy, a rear engine cover, and four wide wheels. The four wheels rotate continuously on their axles, the cockpit canopy rotates upward on a side hinge, and the rear engine cover rotates on a rear horizontal hinge.

A rectangular family waffle iron with a broad lower body, a flat upper lid, and a centered front grip, plus two raised program buttons on the front corner. the lid and base keep clear shell breaks around the cooking plates, and the buttons stay visibly discrete instead of merging into a membrane panel. The lid rotates upward on a rear hinge line, and the two buttons articulate independently as small prismatic push controls. Kitchen counter scale.

A commercial microwave oven with a drop-down front door and a single rotary timer dial. The boxy housing encloses the cavity, a glass tray sits on the cavity floor, and the door is hinged along its lower edge rather than the side. The door rotates on a lower horizontal revolute hinge axis, the timer dial rotates on a front-to-back axis in the control panel, and the turntable rotates on a vertical axis inside the cavity.

Produce an outdoor weatherproof Toy model planes. Use sealed interfaces, drip-aware overhangs, corrosion-resistant hardware, and protected joints, anchored on a model-plane body with wing-tail set and small prop/stand articulation. Keep silhouette readable and practical for fabrication. Propeller/stand articulation should be simple, mounted, and plausible. Require hard mechanical continuity between all components so nothing appears detached. No floating parts.

A kettle-style charcoal grill on a small wheeled stand. A round lower fire bowl supports a domed lid, and the bowl sits in a simple tubular frame with two wheels and a front foot. The lid opens on a rear revolute hinge axis by about 100 degrees, and the two wheels spin continuously on a shared axle.

category_title: Palm with multiple uncoupled finger chains
prompt: A mechanical palm plate with four uncoupled finger mechanisms for an anthropomorphic gripper. The palm is a broad base with finger root knuckles spaced along the front edge and a side-mounted thumb chain, and each digit is a stack of rigid link segments. Each chain uses serial revolute joints with transverse axes; the four fingers have three joints each and the thumb has two, all flexing through about 80 degrees independently.

A desktop guillotine paper cutter. A flat rectangular cutting bed has a fence edge and a raised hinge block at one rear corner, with a long cutter arm spanning diagonally over the bed. The cutter arm rotates downward on a single rear-corner revolute hinge through about 0 to 80 degrees.

category_title: Lounge chair with independent backrest and footrest
prompt: A reclining lounge chair with a fixed seat base, a hinged backrest, and a separate hinged footrest. The seat pan sits on a low support frame, the backrest is attached at the rear side pivots, and the footrest is attached at the front edge on its own linkage-free hinge. The backrest rotates about a transverse revolute axis through roughly 95 to 130 degrees seat-to-back angle, and the footrest rotates upward on a second transverse revolute axis through about 0 to 80 degrees.

A stainless-bodied panini press with chamfered side panels, a lower grill tray, and a domed upper platen with a centered handle. The upper platen rotates on a rear horizontal revolute hinge, a temperature dial rotates continuously on the right side panel, and a removable drip tray translates prismatically out from a slot beneath the front apron. Commercial countertop grill scale.

Create a premium consumer-grade Simple ironing board with elegant proportions and tight surfacing, but keep the mechanics honest: a board deck on scissor legs with a lockable open stance. Use restrained detailing, clean seam strategy, and thoughtful material transitions (painted metal, polymer, elastomer where appropriate). Scissor action and lock points should explain the supported stance. No disconnected pieces; all elements must be physically supported and attached. No floating parts.

A self-erecting tower crane with a square telescoping mast extending on a prismatic joint from a wheeled ground base, a horizontal jib on a slewing vertical revolute joint at the mast top, and a hook-block trolley on prismatic rails along the jib lower chord.

A self-closing push-button washroom faucet with a round pedestal body on a deck plate, a short straight spout, and a mushroom-head push button on a prismatic joint at the body top face that spring-returns to closed after a timed release.
A base-plate slide table. A broad grounded plate supports a long low guide and one compact carriage with a top tooling surface. The carriage is one prismatic joint along the guide direction.

An under-slung rotary shaft unit. A top bracket supports a short shaft between two hanging bearings so the grounded support remains visually distinct from the spinning member. The shaft rotates as one continuous revolute joint about the supported centerline.

A compact pan base with a hex pedestal, a low slewing-ring style turntable, and a square top deck for mounting payloads. The lower support should be clearly static while the top deck reads as the moving stage. One revolute joint yaws the top deck about the vertical centerline.

category_title: Sewing box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A small sewing box with a plain top lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Use a low, wide body with the moving panel spanning most of the width.

A wall-mounted key cabinet with a hinged outer door and a row of twenty mini-hook drawers inside. The outer door opens on a revolute hinge at its vertical left edge. Each internal mini drawer slides prismatically on small guide pins.

An under-slung slide-arm-arm unit. A top support carries a hanging carriage and a two-link arm below it. The hanging carriage is a prismatic joint, and the two links are connected by shoulder and elbow revolute joints.

A compact on-camera flash with a narrow body, a rectangular bounce head, and a low shoe mount, plus a battery-door seam on one side of the body. the proportions stay realistic for a small travel flash while keeping the head joints clear, and the battery door is carried as a distinct panel in the body shell. A small-form-factor HTPC chassis with a slim wide rectangular housing, a front panel door on a right-edge revolute hinge covering drive bays, and a top panel that slides rearward on prismatic rails for tool-less internal access.

A contemporary four-blade metal ceiling fan with a round motor housing on an angled downrod and four curved metal blades. The blade assembly rotates continuously about the motor axis. The downrod attaches to the canopy on a fixed ball-and-socket swivel joint.

A stand mixer in blending mode with a heavy base, a tilting head, and a cylindrical bowl. The mixing head tilts on a revolute hinge at the base head pivot. The bowl locks on a revolute twist-lock at the base. The beater hub rotates continuously.

An overshot waterwheel beside a timber feed chute with a sliding sluice gate. A hand syringe. A cylindrical barrel holds a plunger rod and plunger head, ending in a nozzle tip. Joints: the plunger translates prismatically along the barrel axis. Use fewer, larger structural pieces around the sliding path.

A floor-standing candy vending machine with a tall rectangular cabinet, a glazed front door, spiral product shelves, and a lower retrieval chute. A large-format engineering scanner with a wide rectangular body, a full-width lid on a rear revolute piano hinge, and a media feed roller that rolls print media across the platen — the roller housing flipping up on a front-edge revolute hinge for paper loading.

A knee scooter mobility aid with a padded knee platform deck, two rear wheels on a rear axle revolute joint, two front wheels in a front fork on a steering revolute joint about a vertical axis, and a height-adjustable handlebar post extending on a prismatic joint from the front fork stem.

An under-slung three-axis wrist assembly. A top support carries a yaw stage below it, then a pitch cradle, then a short roll output. The mechanism uses three serial revolute joints on vertical, horizontal, and tool-axis directions.

A cargo bike front fork with a wide curved steel unicrown blade pair and a front rack platform bolted to low-rider mounts at the blades. A threadless straight steerer clamps in a riser stem. Wide flat cargo handlebars clamp at the stem face collar; steering rotates on a revolute head-tube axis.

An H-frame studio easel with two tall front legs and two rear legs, and a horizontal canvas bar that slides up and down the front legs. The canvas bar slides prismatically on the front legs via two locking slide brackets. The top clamp bar also slides prismatically.

A hot-shoe flash with a boxy lamp head, a short articulated neck, and a rear control panel below the display, plus a bounce card sliding from the head top. the head and neck stay visibly separate from the battery body, and the bounce card remains a distinct insert inside the head shell. The neck swivels on a vertical revolute joint above the body and the flash head tilts on a horizontal revolute joint at the top of the neck, and the bounce card slides prismatically out from the head top slot. A freestanding gas stove with five burners including a central oval burner and a lower oven door. Five rotary knobs form a shallow arc across the front rail beneath the cooktop overhang. Each knob rotates continuously about a front-to-back revolute axis, and the oven door opens downward on a lower horizontal hinge axis.

A rack-mount 4U desktop tower chassis with a wide low-profile rectangular body, a hinged front bezel panel on a revolute hinge at the left edge, and a top access panel on two rear revolute hinge pins that lifts for internal access.

A pair of flip-up driving glasses with a main frame, two temple arms, and a tinted secondary lens frame mounted ahead of the primary rims. The tinted front is carried by two small hinges near the brow line, while the temples connect at the outer corners of the main frame. The temples fold on their usual outer revolute hinges, and the tinted front rotates upward on a transverse pair of revolute pivots above the lenses.

A compact service axis with a hinged extension nose. A fixed rail supports a moving carriage, the carriage carries a hinged frame, and the hinged frame contains a short rectangular ram. The carriage slides on a prismatic joint, the frame rotates on a revolute joint, and the ram slides on a distal prismatic joint.

A low-profile joystick mechanism for an equipment console. A circular base cup carries a cardan gimbal and a short upright lever capped by a small clamp block. The outer ring pitches about a left-right axis and the inner ring rolls about a front-back axis, both around 18 degrees each way.

Create a premium consumer-grade Windshield sun visor with elegant proportions and tight surfacing, but keep the mechanics honest: a visor panel on a roof hinge arm plus secondary swing pivot. Use restrained detailing, clean seam strategy, and thoughtful material transitions (painted metal, polymer, elastomer where appropriate). Primary and secondary pivots should be visible and mechanically credible. No disconnected pieces; all elements must be physically supported and attached. No floating parts.

Produce an outdoor weatherproof Searchlight tower. Use sealed interfaces, drip-aware overhangs, corrosion-resistant hardware, and protected joints, anchored on a mast/tower support carrying a spotlight head in a pan-tilt yoke. Keep silhouette readable and practical for fabrication. Pan and tilt axes should be separated and carried by yoke/bearing blocks. Require hard mechanical continuity between all components so nothing appears detached. No floating parts.

A toddler bucket swing on a steel playground frame. The deep seat is hung from two side support straps connected to the top beam through short hanger joints. The bucket seat swings on the hanger pivots at the top beam supports.

A compact turbojet engine module with an intake lip, cylindrical core body, and conical exhaust nozzle. The outer casing is supported by front struts around a visible compressor rotor near the intake. The visible rotor rotates continuously about the engine centerline revolute axis.

A traditional five-blade wooden ceiling fan with a round canopy, a short downrod, a cylindrical motor housing, and five flat rectangular blades on blade irons. The downrod is fixed to the canopy. The motor housing and blade irons rotate continuously about the vertical axis.

A benchtop teaching microscope with a broad base, a monocular tube, an objective turret, and a mechanical stage with a side carriage, plus a second stage-axis control on the stage side. the mechanical stage remains clearly separate from the supporting base and arm, and the two axis controls sit as distinct coaxial knobs on the stage bracket. The objective turret rotates continuously on the nosepiece axis, the optical body slides prismatically on the focusing column, and the side carriage slides prismatically along the stage guide, and the second stage control rotates continuously on its local axis. A food processor with a wide rectangular base and a wide cylindrical bowl that locks onto the base. The bowl locks on a revolute bayonet joint. A disc lid on the bowl opens on a revolute hinge at the lid edge. The inner blade rotates continuously.

An overshot waterwheel with a side-mounted drive pinion on the same axle. The large bucketed wheel sits between support blocks, and a smaller gear wheel is fixed to one axle end outside the main rim. The waterwheel rotates continuously on its horizontal axle, and the side pinion rotates continuously with the same axle.

A hand syringe. A cylindrical barrel holds a plunger rod and plunger head, ending in a nozzle tip. Joints: the plunger translates prismatically along the barrel axis. Break the assembly into base frame, guide module, and moving carriage modules.

Create a cost-optimized mass-manufacturable Wind turbine. Base architecture: a tower with nacelle, rotor hub, and blade-root interfaces. Reduce part count, simplify molds/extrusions/stampings, and keep assembly order obvious with realistic snap/bolt/clamp interfaces. Rotor and blade roots should be hub-mounted with realistic load paths. Every piece must have a believable attachment path; no floating or orphaned components. No floating parts.

A floor-standing globe with a large sphere carried in a partial meridian above a pedestal turntable. The sphere is mounted on polar pivots inside the meridian, and the whole support stands on a circular base that can swivel. The globe spins on its polar revolute axis, and the stand rotates on a vertical revolute axis at the base turntable.

A narrow tower candy vending machine with stacked candy spirals behind a glass front, a control panel at mid height, and a cash drawer at the base. The cash drawer translates prismatically on straight runners, the retrieval flap rotates on a horizontal hinge at the pickup opening, and a selector dial on the control panel rotates continuously about its shaft.

A transparency backlight scanner with a rectangular body, a hinged lid that opens on a rear revolute hinge with an integrated backlight panel inside, and a film strip holder tray sliding on prismatic guide rails into a slot at the body front.

A foldable electric commuter scooter with a flat board deck, front and rear pneumatic wheels on revolute axles, a telescoping handlebar stem extending on a prismatic joint from the neck, and a fold mechanism at the stem base on a revolute hinge with a hook latch that clips the stem to the rear fender.

A corner-cabinet lazy Susan with two round shelves on a central post. The post is anchored between a base plate and a top support, and the shelves are fixed to the rotating center shaft. The shelf assembly rotates about the vertical revolute axis of the central shaft relative to the cabinet base.

A compact articulating TV wall mount shown without any display attached. A short wall bracket supports a primary arm, a secondary arm, and a tilting faceplate on separate knuckle blocks. The two arm joints are revolutes about vertical axes, the faceplate swivels slightly about a vertical axis, and the tilt hinge rotates about a horizontal axis.

category_title: Laptop stand with articulated height adjustment
