The braces are matched supports for the same tray and stay aligned with one another. The spine extends prismatically along a vertical guide, and the two braces rotate on paired horizontal pivots at the spine and tray corners while the tray remains rigid.

A long telephoto zoom lens with a rotating tripod collar and a zoom barrel. The collar ring rotates on a revolute joint about the lens body axis for landscape or portrait orientation. The zoom barrel rotates on a revolute joint about the optical axis for focal length.

Create a cost-optimized mass-manufacturable Usb drive with swivel cover. Base architecture: a USB drive body with connector and side-pinned swivel cover. Reduce part count, simplify molds/extrusions/stampings, and keep assembly order obvious with realistic snap/bolt/clamp interfaces. Swivel cover movement should be constrained by a visible pivot pin. Every piece must have a believable attachment path; no floating or orphaned components. No floating parts.

Create a cost-optimized mass-manufacturable Stick vacuum with folding joint. Base architecture: a slim vacuum body with folding wand joint and floor head. Reduce part count, simplify molds/extrusions/stampings, and keep assembly order obvious with realistic snap/bolt/clamp interfaces. Fold joint and nozzle articulation should be pinned and bracketed. Every piece must have a believable attachment path; no floating or orphaned components. No floating parts.

A computerized goto reflector on a single-arm fork mount. A Newtonian reflector tube mounts in a ring cradle on a revolute altitude joint in the fork arm. The fork arm rotates continuously on an azimuth motor bearing on a flat tripod head.

A commercial-style wall hood with removable baffle filters and a row of control knobs. The canopy body is fixed, the baffle-filter frame is carried on a hinged access panel, and the control knobs sit across the front fascia. The access panel rotates on its hinge under the canopy, and the control knobs rotate on their own local shafts.

A floor-standing heavy drill press with a round column, a fixed base, a height-adjustable tilting table, and a quill-feed spindle head. The table slides prismatically up and down the column. The table tilts on a revolute hinge at the table mount bracket. The quill drops on a prismatic joint inside the head.

A shoulder-style mini camcorder with a rounded rear battery bulge, a forward lens tube, and a side LCD panel recessed into the left wall. The LCD panel swings open on a vertical revolute hinge, a tape-compartment door rotates downward on a horizontal hinge below the display opening, and a selector dial rotates continuously on the top deck behind the handle. Portable recording-device scale.

A domestic rice cooker with a rounded body, a rear-hinged lid, and a front control area below the latch, plus a front latch button and two separate menu buttons. the outer shell reads as a compact countertop appliance with a clear lid seam and steam vent, and the buttons remain visibly discrete parts in the front panel. The lid rotates upward on a rear horizontal hinge, and the latch button translates prismatically into the body and the two menu buttons depress independently. Compact kitchen-appliance scale.

category_title: Pedal trash bin with hinged lid
prompt: A cylindrical stainless steel step bin with a domed lid and a narrow pedal bar. The round body carries the lid on a rear hinge band, with the pedal bar mounted low on the front wall. The lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge line around the rim, and the pedal bar rotates on a small transverse pivot near the lower front edge.

A yaw-pitch-roll wrist. A base ring carries a pitch yoke, and the yoke carries a roll spindle ending in a small tool flange. Yaw rotates about the vertical axis roughly 150 degrees each way, pitch rotates about a horizontal trunnion roughly 60 degrees each way, and roll rotates about the tool axis roughly 180 degrees each way.

A mountain bike air-suspension fork with a wide crown, two telescoping lower stanchion legs each extending on a prismatic joint from upper inner tubes, a tapered threadless steerer clamped in a two-bolt stem, and wide flat mountain handlebars in the stem clamp. The steering rotates on the head-tube revolute axis.

A folding A-frame step ladder. A front climbing frame and a rear support frame meet at a top cap, with wide treads on the front frame. Joints: the rear frame folds relative to the front frame on a single revolute hinge at the top cap. Break the frame into obvious left rail, right rail, and top-bracket modules.

Model Robotic leg as an industrial safety-first variant for heavy duty cycles. Start from a hip-knee-ankle chain with load-bearing limb shells and actuator bays, then add guards, lockouts, over-travel stops, and reinforcement at high-stress joints. Favor visible fastener logic and plate/brace load paths over decorative surfacing. Show leg articulation as true serial kinematics with plausible range limits. All subparts must remain connected to a parent support; no unsupported fragments. No floating parts.

A tall reel-style slot machine with a curved top box, a central reel window, a front button shelf, and a lower service door. The button shelf projects from the cabinet face as a real ledge and the lower door remains recessed into the cabinet front. The lower service door rotates on a vertical side hinge, and the play buttons on the shelf articulate independently as small prismatic push controls. Full-height slot-machine scale.

A bathroom wall-mount air purifier with a slim rectangular housing on a wall bracket, a hinged front cover that swings open on two revolute knuckle hinges at the cover right edge, and a washable filter tray inside on a prismatic slide at the housing base.

A chunky snap-off utility knife with a segmented blade and a thumb slider on top. The outer body is a straight channel shell with a nose opening for the blade, and a small lock wheel is mounted near the slider on the housing side. The blade carrier moves prismatically along the body axis, and the lock wheel rotates on a short transverse revolute axis through the shell.

A hard-shell violin case with a clamshell body. A shallow lower shell holds the cavity and a matching upper shell forms the lid, with toggle latches spaced along the front edge and a handle between them. The lid rotates on a long rear revolute hinge axis, and each front latch rotates on its own small pivot to hook and release the lid.

An e-bike torque-sensing crankset with a wide cylindrical bottom-bracket shell, two crank arms on the spindle ends, and a large chainring. The spindle and both crank arms rotate continuously about the bottom-bracket axis. Each pedal spindle rotates continuously about its own axis.

A wide inspection portal with box-section legs, a rigid crossbeam, a central shuttle under the beam, and a narrow vertical tool carriage below the shuttle. The shuttle slides prismatically across the beam span, and the lower carriage slides prismatically in the vertical direction.

A larger insulated pet flap with a thick outer frame and a clear central door panel. The flap is supported near its upper corners by pivot pins inside the frame hood, and a latch tab is mounted at the lower edge. The flap rotates on a horizontal top revolute axis, and the latch tab rotates on a small local pivot at the flap edge.

A compact knee walker with a front steering column and a height-adjustable knee pad platform. The chassis has two rear wheels on a straight axle and two front caster-like support wheels carried by the steering fork assembly. The wheels rotate continuously on their axles, and the front assembly steers on a vertical revolute axis with about +/-40 degrees of range.

A marine deck hatch with a thick rectangular frame and a slightly raised lid. The lid overlaps the fixed curb frame and has side latch blocks near the front corners. A rear-edge revolute hinge axis lets the lid lift about 75 degrees.

A dock loading ramp with a wide steel deck, a hinged lip at the front edge, and two folding support leg pairs mounted under the deck. The lip and leg pairs remain visibly supported by real hinge brackets beneath the deck structure. The main deck rotates on a rear horizontal storage hinge, the front lip rotates on a horizontal hinge at the deck edge, and the support leg pairs rotate on their underside hinge pins. Loading-bay ramp scale.

An air mouse remote with an ergonomic oval-grip body and a rotating jog dial wheel on the top face. The jog dial rotates continuously about a horizontal axis. A battery cover on the underside pivots on a revolute hinge at the cover's short edge.

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: An open-frame two-branch motion study. A grounded central post carries two arms on individual hub collars, and the arm tips use different simple end shapes such as pads, forks, or tabs to add believable variation. Each arm is an independent revolute joint carried by its own hub support.

A bridge-mounted lever chain. A fixed bridge support carries a root clevis and a sequence of links whose section sizes taper toward the distal small end tab. The chain has three revolute joints in series.

A fork-backed arm with telescoping nose. A rear support captures the first arm link, followed by a second link and a short output extension. The two arm links articulate through revolute joints, and the extension is a prismatic joint.

A convertible hand truck with a fold-out secondary toe section. The main tubular frame carries two large rear wheels and a primary nose plate, while a hinged extension plate nests under the main plate. The two wheels rotate continuously on the axle, and the extension plate folds on a revolute joint under the main toe plate through about 180 degrees.
A smart washer with a dark charcoal body, a drum on a revolute axle, and a large circular door with a stainless ring bezel hinged on the left edge.

A compact mini food processor with a short motor base, a clear bowl, a lid with a narrow cylindrical feed tube, and a simple front control cluster made of a timer knob and two raised mode buttons. The feed pusher slides prismatically inside the tube, the chopping blade rotates continuously on a short central spindle, the timer knob rotates continuously on the front face, and the two mode buttons move independently as small prismatic push controls. A utility blimp with a thick envelope, a compact gondola, twin engine pods, and a boxy tail cruciform, plus a small wheeled landing gear under the gondola. the gondola hangs from visible support struts below the hull centerline, and the gear remains a real supported undercarriage attached to the gondola structure. The propellers rotate continuously on their pod shafts, the rudder rotates on a vertical hinge line in the rear fin, and the elevators rotate on horizontal hinges in the tailplanes, and the landing wheels rotate continuously on their axle. A tall rolling toolbox with a narrow upright body, two recessed transport wheels, and a telescoping pull handle hidden in a rear spine channel. Build a precision professional Tilting fan tuned for calibration and repeatable alignment. A low-profile remote weapon station with a rotating base, an elevation cradle, and a side sensor pod. A premium countertop stand mixer with a broad heel, a medium bowl on a low slide carriage, and a tidy rounded head fitted with a whisk. A drop-down pickup tailgate with an inner fold-down work surface panel. The main gate rotates on a lower horizontal hinge axis, the work surface panel rotates on its own horizontal hinge axis within the gate, and the latch handle rotates on its local axis at the outer face. Keep the inner work-surface panel clipped to its hinge inside the main gate so the smaller panel stays attached while folding out.

A double-sided display easel with a central telescoping upright column on a prismatic joint from a wide foot base, two angled side display tray arms each on a revolute hinge at the column top facing opposite directions, and two top clip rails each on a revolute hinge above each tray arm.

A compact two-axis instrument head. A pedestal supports an outer ring or fork, and the outer member carries an inner cradle with a simple flange at the center. The outer axis is a revolute roll joint and the inner axis is a revolute pitch joint on a perpendicular support.

Design a retrofit/legacy-inspired Undershot waterwheel that blends old-school construction cues with modern reliability. A pedestal-mounted missile launcher with a boxy launch pod. A fixed base supports a yaw turntable, which carries a tilting cradle with a rectangular missile canister pack mounted on top. The launcher yaws continuously about a vertical revolute axis at the base and pitches on a horizontal side revolute axis through about -10 to +60 degrees.

A countertop centrifugal juicer with a rounded base housing, a clear upper lid, and a cylindrical feed pusher that slides vertically in the top chute. A rotary speed selector is mounted on the front of the base, and the spinning filter basket sits inside the chamber below the lid. The feed pusher moves prismatically along the chute axis, the lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge, the speed selector rotates on its own short shaft, and the filter basket rotates continuously about the vertical central axis.

A box metronome with a hinged top lid that conceals the winding key. A rectangular upright housing on a flat base carries a pendulum rod with a cylindrical sliding weight from an upper internal pivot; the lid opens rearward on a horizontal revolute hinge at the housing top, revealing a winding key set into the lid face. The pendulum pivots on a revolute joint at the upper shaft; the weight slides prismatically; the lid opens on its rear hinge; the key turns continuously.

A desktop Wi-Fi router with four external antennas. A low rectangular housing carries four identical antenna stalks mounted at the rear corners and near the centerline, with small feet under the base. Each antenna attaches through a single revolute hinge at its root and can tilt in a vertical plane through about 120 degrees.

Design a retrofit/legacy-inspired Traditional windmill that blends old-school construction cues with modern reliability. A compact console monitor arm without the monitor. A grounded base bracket supports two short articulated links and a small head frame that can fold close to the base. Two revolute joints fold the arm, and the head adds separate swivel and tilt revolute joints.

A compact speaker with a hinged protective cover over the controls. The small speaker body has a narrow lid over the top control strip and a short carry handle at one side. The control cover rotates on a small hinge at the top edge, and the carry handle rotates on its side pivots at the case corner.

Design Robotic arms as a field-service workhorse. Build it around a base pedestal feeding a shoulder-elbow-wrist chain with clear joint cartridges. Prioritize chunky hardware, replaceable wear parts, clear maintenance access, and robust tolerances for repeated use. Make axis order and joint offsets readable so motion paths are unambiguous. Every part must be connected by real brackets, shafts, hinges, rails, collars, weldments, or housings; absolutely no floating geometry. No floating parts.

Create a premium consumer-grade Stationary exercise bike with elegant proportions and tight surfacing, but keep the mechanics honest: a bike frame with crank set, flywheel housing, saddle post, and bars. Use restrained detailing, clean seam strategy, and thoughtful material transitions (painted metal, polymer, elastomer where appropriate). Crank and adjusters should be mounted on explicit bearing/clamp features. No disconnected pieces; all elements must be physically supported and attached. No floating parts.

A fixed-wing VTOL hybrid drone with a main wing body and two tilt-rotor nacelles at the wing tips. Each nacelle tilts on a revolute hinge at the wing tip about a spanwise horizontal axis. Each rotor propeller in the nacelle spins continuously about its own axis.

A quick-release bench vise with flat jaws and a side-mounted release lever. The moving jaw slides prismatically along twin guide bars; a release lever pivots on a revolute joint at the vise body side to disengage the lead-screw half-nut and allow free jaw repositioning; the lead-screw handle rotates continuously for fine adjustment.

category_title: Top-load washing machine with hinged lid
prompt: A modern top-load washer with a rounded lid, a right-side detergent drawer in the top deck, and a rear control tower with a selector knob and three option buttons. The lid rotates upward on a rear hinge, the detergent drawer slides prismatically out from the top deck, and the wash basket rotates continuously on the vertical axis. The selector knob rotates continuously, and the three buttons are separate prismatic push controls. The basket and cabinet opening should stay clearly hollow with believable depth for clothing. Residential laundry scale.

category_title: Desktop pencil sharpener with hand crank and drawer
prompt: A cast-metal classroom pencil sharpener with a boxy body, a side hand crank, a front shavings drawer, and a size-selector dial around the pencil port, plus a free-spinning knob at the crank tip. The pencil entry remains a true hollow opening into the cutter cavity and the crank knob is clearly mounted on the handle arm. The hand crank rotates continuously on its supported side axle, the shavings drawer slides prismatically out from the front of the housing, the size-selector dial rotates continuously around the pencil-entry axis, and the crank-tip knob rotates continuously on its own local axle. Classroom desktop scale.

category_title: Adjustable weight bench with hinged backrest
prompt: A folding adjustable weight bench with a low tubular frame, a split backrest and seat pad, a rear support link, and front transport wheels, plus a foot-roller assembly at the front end. the pads sit on separate support brackets over a compact home-gym frame, and the roller pair is mounted on a rigid front yoke. The backrest pivots on a transverse hinge relative to the center frame, the seat pad pivots on its own hinge ahead of the backrest, the rear support link rotates on a lower frame pivot, and the front wheels spin continuously on their axle, and the two front rollers spin continuously on their shared support axle. Compact home-gym scale.

A pair of over-ear headphones with a padded headband and two circular earcups. Each earcup is carried by a short yoke at the end of the headband arm, and the yoke includes a fold hinge near the cup mount. Each cup folds inward on a revolute hinge through about 90 degrees, and the cup can swivel slightly on a second local revolute axis within the yoke.

A desktop ring light on a compact stand with a weighted base. The circular light head is attached to a short yoke on top of a telescoping mast rising from the base. The head tilts on a horizontal revolute axis through about +/-45 degrees, and the mast extends prismatically along the vertical axis by about 150 mm.

A toy coupe with a short hood, a two-door body, a rear trunk volume, and four exposed wheels, plus a rear trunk lid instead of a hatchback. the body keeps clear door cuts and a simple trunk seam, and the trunk lid stays visibly separate from the rear body deck. The four wheels rotate continuously on their axles and the two side doors rotate outward on vertical hinges at the A-pillars, and the trunk lid rotates upward on a short rear-deck hinge line. Small die-cast toy-car scale.

A square clamshell waffle maker with thick upper and lower plate housings, a short insulated front handle, and a rear hinge block, plus a thermostat dial on the side wall. the upper shell stays clearly supported by the rear hinge instead of floating above the lower body, and the dial remains a separate control on the housing side. The top plate assembly lifts on a single horizontal hinge at the back, and the thermostat dial rotates continuously on its short shaft. Countertop waffle-maker scale.

A tall single-handle kitchen mixer faucet with a round pedestal body on a deck plate, a high-arc gooseneck spout neck, and a single side joystick lever handle on a revolute joint at the body waist that pivots for temperature and lifts for flow rate.

A drawer-like instrument slide with a fixed base channel, a traveling carriage tray, and a blocky front stop feature. The base and carriage should read as one clean guided pair with enough overlap that the moving tray never feels unsupported. Make it feel real with machined guide faces, wear strips, stop blocks, and grounded plate thickness throughout the base and carriage. The carriage moves on a single prismatic axis along the channel direction. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A compact shaft fixture with exposed bearings. A short frame holds a supported shaft, a midspan collar, and a square drive plate so the moving member stays legible as one rigid rotary part. One continuous revolute joint rotates the shaft about its own axis inside the fixed supports.

An under-slung pan stage. A top beam supports a hanging rotary head with a small tooling plate below it, making the fixed support read above the moving member. One revolute joint turns the tooling plate about the vertical support axis.

category_title: Sewing box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A small sewing box with a plain top lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Add one fixed stop flange or secondary frame lip around the opening.
A library card catalog cabinet with a tall rectangular carcass and forty-eight small index card drawers in a grid. Each drawer slides prismatically on individual wooden guide rails.

An under-cabinet LED strip lamp with a flat wall bracket, a two-link folding arm, and a flat LED strip bar at the tip. The first link attaches to the wall bracket on a revolute joint. The second link pivots on a revolute elbow. The LED bar rotates on a revolute joint at the terminal axis.

A single-sash sliding window. A rectangular outer frame contains one fixed frame segment and one sliding sash. Joints: the sash translates prismatically along horizontal guide channels. Use fewer, larger structural pieces around the sliding path.

A compact roll stage with a rectangular base frame, two aligned end supports, and a cylindrical spindle tube spanning between the supports. The fixed end supports should visibly ground the spindle so the roll axis never reads as a floating cylinder. Use realistic detail such as clamp collars, cap screws, support feet, and practical shaft overhang at the two ends of the rotating member. The rotating tube is a single revolute stage about the supported roll axis. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

An offset inspection hatch hinge with a short mounting leaf, a taller moving leaf, and a spacer-like barrel that keeps the leaves apart. The hinge geometry should clearly show how the moving leaf sweeps around the pin. The assembly uses one revolute joint about the hinge barrel axis.

A weatherproof swivel bar stool with a weighted pedestal, a perforated metal seat, and a fold-down backrest pad. The seat rotates continuously about the vertical pedestal axis, and the backrest pad rotates downward on a short rear hinge. Outdoor bar scale.

A clean-room style lift axis with twin round posts, a compact base can, a narrow top bridge, and a precise carriage block carrying a small rectangular table. The moving carriage should show paired bearing sleeves or guide blocks on the two posts so the support is explicit from every view. The assembly should feel like real precision equipment with polished guide posts, machined end plates, trim covers only where needed, and practical top-and-bottom stops. One prismatic joint lifts the carriage vertically along the post direction. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A two-axis positioning stage. A base carries an X slide with a rectangular carriage, and that carriage supports a Y slide turned ninety degrees with a small top table. The X and Y stages are prismatic joints along orthogonal horizontal axes.

A die-handling lift mast with box-section uprights, a broad faceplate carriage, and two stub load arms under a tall back frame. The moving carriage should look heavier and wider than a warehouse pallet carriage, with guide hardware occupying real space against the uprights. The assembly should feel like real industrial lifting hardware with welded channels, bolted rollers, reinforced fork roots, and practical stop geometry. The carriage travels on one vertical prismatic joint along the mast guides. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A louver-style vane bank. A rectangular frame holds several parallel slats, each supported at both ends by its own pivot stubs. Each vane is an independent revolute joint about its long axis within the side frame.

A screw-in light bulb seated in a socket. A bulb envelope sits above a threaded metal base, and the socket body holds the mating threaded collar. Joints: the bulb rotates continuously about the shared socket axis. Add one fixed mounting flange, foot, or wall plate as a secondary support feature.

A transportable satellite dish with a medium reflector on a boxy azimuth base. The rear dish frame carries the feed arm and sits in an elevation yoke above a squat rotating base with a side crank. The base rotates about a vertical azimuth axis, the dish frame rotates about a horizontal elevation axis through the yoke, and the side crank rotates on its own local axis at the base. Keep the rear dish frame clipped in the elevation yoke above the boxy base so the reflector stays supported during tilt.

category_title: Desktop monitor with tilt swivel stand
prompt: A business monitor with a boxy display shell, a telescoping stand neck, and a rounded pedestal base, plus a rear cable-cover door on the stand spine. the shell stays slightly thicker than an ultrathin entertainment screen so the support geometry reads clearly, and the cable door is a real supported flap rather than a seam line. The screen tilts on a horizontal hinge at the stand head, the stand swivels continuously on a vertical axis above the base, and the neck slides prismatically for height adjustment, and the cable-cover door rotates on a small hinge along one vertical edge. Small office-monitor scale.

A column-mounted radial-arm module. A fixed post carries a rotary turret with a straight beam, and the beam supports a traveling truck on top or beneath it. The turret is a vertical revolute joint and the truck is a prismatic joint along the beam axis.

Produce an outdoor weatherproof Wheelie bin with hinged lid. Use sealed interfaces, drip-aware overhangs, corrosion-resistant hardware, and protected joints, anchored on a wheeled bin shell with rear axle and full-width hinged lid. Keep silhouette readable and practical for fabrication. Lid swing and wheel rolling should both be clearly supported. Require hard mechanical continuity between all components so nothing appears detached. No floating parts.

A simple two-link elbow arm. A fixed base carries an upper arm link, and the upper arm supports a forearm link ending in a small tool pad. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints about parallel horizontal axes.

A compact plunger-and-lever mechanism. A fixed guide body carries a moving rod with a squared tip, and the tip engages a small hinged tab at the front face. The rod slides on a prismatic joint and the tab rotates on a revolute joint.

A service pole with telescoping lift and pan stage. A grounded foot carries stacked telescoping sleeves, and the top sleeve supports a compact rotating platform for a sensor or fixture. The sleeves are vertical prismatic joints and the platform is a revolute joint on the top stage.

A countertop toaster oven with a rectangular body, a front drop-down door, and three rotary control knobs at one side of the face. The door rotates downward on a lower horizontal hinge at the front opening, and the three knobs rotate continuously on their short front-facing shafts. Countertop appliance scale.

A wall-backed vertical axis with tilt head. A rigid backplate carries a vertical guide and a moving front carriage with a short hinged output tab. The carriage slides on a vertical prismatic joint and the output tab rotates on a revolute joint mounted to the carriage.

A boxed industrial four-joint revolute chain. A reinforced base cheek supports deeper-section links with visible pin bosses, and the final link terminates in a compact end tab. The assembly is a serial chain of four revolute joints about parallel hinge axes.

A cantilever articulated arm for bench tooling. A grounded column carries a shoulder link, an elbow link, and a short wrist tool plate, with the whole arm projecting to one side of the support. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints about parallel horizontal axes, and the wrist is a third revolute joint at the tool end.

A broad-knuckle articulated finger. A palm-side base block supports three tapered digit segments that decrease in size toward the tip. The three digit segments are joined by three revolute joints about parallel knuckle axes.

Design a retrofit/legacy-inspired Vacuum cleaner with articulated wand that blends old-school construction cues with modern reliability. Keep the core as a vacuum body with articulated wand segments and floor nozzle; include service hatches, bolted adapters, and pragmatic reinforcements where components meet. Wand/nozzle articulation should be jointed with collars and pivots. The final model must be one connected mechanism where every element is structurally supported. No floating parts.

category_title: CCTV mast with pan-tilt camera head
prompt: A double-arm corner-mount CCTV bracket with a rectangular corner wall plate, two adjustable side arms each on a revolute hinge at the plate face, and a camera head on a pan-tilt yoke at each arm tip with a vertical pan revolute and horizontal tilt revolute.

category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A pedestal-mounted three-branch tooling tree. A heavy foot and vertical column carry three rotary arms on discrete clamp blocks, and each arm ends in a small rectangular faceplate. Each branch rotates by a revolute joint at its own support block, with no shared linkage between branches.

A compact drafting stand with a pedestal base, a telescoping center post, and a medium rectangular board with a lower lip, plus a shallow accessory drawer under the board. the telescoping post remains visibly nested rather than reading as one solid mast, and the drawer is carried by short runners below the board frame. The center post slides prismatically for height adjustment and the board tilts on a horizontal hinge at the top support, and the accessory drawer slides prismatically on its guide runners. Small studio drafting scale.
A countertop bar chest cooler with a compact rectangular body and a hinged lid with a chrome rail frame, pivoting on two rear knuckle revolute hinges and secured by a flip latch at the front center.

A wall-backed articulated transfer nose. A rigid backplate carries a hinged carrier, the carrier contains a sliding member, and the sliding member ends in a short rotating tool tab. The carrier rotates on a revolute joint, the middle member translates prismatically, and the tip tab rotates on a second revolute joint.

category_title: Tackle box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A tackle box with a plain hinged lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge, with internal tray details kept fixed inside the body. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Make the lid or door long and thin relative to the body.

category_title: Standing desk with synchronous telescoping legs and articulated controls
prompt: A bench-style standing desk with a long top, three lifting columns, and a front paddle controller beneath the center edge, plus a shallow keyboard tray under the front edge. the three telescoping columns clearly support the same rigid desktop frame, and the tray is mounted on short side guides below the top frame. All three inner leg stages slide prismatically inside their outer columns in sync, and the keyboard tray slides prismatically on its guides. Shared-workstation desk scale.

A field telescope mounted on a tripod with three folding legs, a sliding center column, and a tilt head. The tripod legs rotate on crown hinges, the center column slides prismatically within the tripod body, the head pans on a vertical joint, and the telescope body tilts on a horizontal hinge at the head cradle. Outdoor observation scale.

A single-leaf drawbridge. A fixed shore frame carries one bridge leaf on heavy side bearings. Joints: the bridge leaf is a single revolute panel about a horizontal shore-side axis. Offset the panel opening so the fixed body is heavier on one side.

A drafting office chair with a tall gas column, a circular foot ring, and flip-up armrests. The seat and back are carried by the center post above the wheeled base, and each armrest is mounted to the seat side on a hinge. The caster wheels spin on their axles, the caster forks swivel on vertical pivots, the seat swivels on the center column, the center column slides prismatically for height adjustment, the back reclines on a transverse hinge, and each armrest rotates upward on its own side hinge.

An orthogonal YZ stage. A grounded back frame carries a horizontal Y slide, and the Y carriage supports a vertical Z slide with a small hanging tool plate. The Y carriage and the hanging Z carriage are prismatic joints on orthogonal horizontal and vertical axes.

A swing-out slide mechanism. A base bracket carries a hinged primary arm, and the free end of that arm supports a short linear slide with a rectangular carriage. The first joint is revolute about the base pin axis, and the second joint is prismatic along the slide axis mounted to the arm.

A bench-top folding arm chain. A base foot supports a stack of slim links with clear side plates at the joints, and the distal link ends in a compact end pad. There are three revolute joints in sequence, each supported by a local hinge block and aligned to the same motion plane.

A compact cradle indexer with a circular lower stage, an offset support frame, and a round faceplate carried in the frame. The upper faceplate should feel like a real trunnion-mounted fixture head with enough surrounding structure to explain the support and sweep. Use realistic detail such as bearing covers, cheek plates, stop features, clamp rings, and practical gaps between the lower stage and the upper cradle. The lower stage rotates about a vertical axis and the upper faceplate rotates about a horizontal axis through the cradle. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A screw-cap bottle. A bottle body rises to a threaded neck, capped by a separate screw closure. Joints: the cap rotates continuously about the bottle neck axis. Keep the axis hardware tucked into the body.

A gear-ring fidget with a flat disc body and three outer spur gear rings on revolute axles around the disc perimeter, meshing with a central sun gear on a revolute joint at the disc center for synchronized spinning.

A bariatric overbed table with a wide H-base on casters, twin lift posts, a broad meal tray, and a release paddle below the work surface, plus a low foot-operated brake bar across the base. the two lift posts clearly support the same tray assembly as matched members, and the brake bar bridges the lower frame rails as a real supported part. Both inner lift posts slide prismatically within their outer columns, the tray tilts on a transverse hinge at the top carriage, and all four casters spin continuously on their axles, and the brake bar rotates on short pivots at the base frame. Wide patient-room scale.

A steam combi-oven with a wide rectangular stainless body, a glass-panel door on a bottom revolute hinge with a chrome bar handle, a water reservoir drawer on a prismatic joint at the left body side, and a top control panel cover on a revolute hinge at the panel rear edge for service access.

category_title: Wall Safe With Hinged Door And Dial
prompt: A wall-mounted safe with a broad front flange, a right-hinged main door, a combination dial, and a small deposit flap above the handle. The main door rotates on a vertical hinge at the frame side, the combination dial rotates continuously about its central axis, and the deposit flap rotates on a top horizontal hinge in the door face.

A perforated rehearsal music stand with a flat plate base, a telescoping post, and a shallow punched-metal desk with two small page clips along the top edge. The clips remain separate parts on the desk rim and the desk head stays visibly distinct from the mast. The post translates prismatically within the lower tube, the desk rotates on a horizontal tilt bracket, and the two page clips rotate on short pivots. Practice-room stand scale.
An under-slung telescoping beam. A top support carries several nested rectangular sections below it instead of above a base. The nested sections extend through serial prismatic joints along one axis.

A medieval stone-throwing bombard with a wide squat cast-iron barrel in a fixed wooden bed cradle, the barrel pivoting on a horizontal elevation revolute joint at the cradle side bracket cheeks, and a hinged touch-hole cover plate on a revolute hinge at the barrel breech top.

A wall thermostat with a large dial. A wall plate carries a shallow body and a central dial ring. Joints: the dial rotates continuously about the center axis. Make the rotating element broad and shallow.

A right-angle gear assembly. A base bracket supports a vertical shaft with a bevel gear and a horizontal shaft with the mating bevel gear, plus clear bearing blocks on both shafts. The vertical and horizontal shafts are separate revolute joints about their own supported axes.

A manual three-wing revolving door with an exposed round center column. The column carries three equally-spaced rectangular wings reaching to the drum wall. The wing assembly rotates continuously about the vertical column axis.

A low-profile wireless keyboard with a fold-out tablet stand along the rear edge. The slim tray-like body supports a full field of short keycaps, and a narrow support flap is recessed into the top rear edge behind the function row. The keys move as short vertical prismatic plungers, and the stand flap rotates upward on a long horizontal hinge axis at the rear edge. Keep the fold-out stand flap clipped to its rear hinge line so it stays attached while propping the tablet slot open.

A fairground observation wheel with an A-frame support and open gondola seats. The large spoked wheel stands between two side supports, and the seats hang from pivots around the rim. The main wheel rotates continuously about its horizontal axle, and each gondola rotates freely on its own hanger pivot so it can stay level. Keep each gondola seat clipped to its rim hanger pivot so the seat stays captured while the main wheel turns.

Produce an outdoor weatherproof Simple drying rack. Use sealed interfaces, drip-aware overhangs, corrosion-resistant hardware, and protected joints, anchored on a foldable rack with wing frames, hinge links, and hanging rails. Keep silhouette readable and practical for fabrication. Wing folding should follow clear hinge lines and stop positions. Require hard mechanical continuity between all components so nothing appears detached. No floating parts.

A tall locker cabinet with one vented sheet-metal door and a rotary latch handle. The rectangular cabinet body stands on short feet, and the full-height door is attached to one side through exposed barrel hinges. The door swings on a vertical revolute hinge axis, and the latch handle rotates on its own local axis at mid height. Keep the full-height metal door clipped into the exposed barrel hinges so the latch side does not drift away from the locker body.

An electric tilt-only moonroof cassette with an aluminium frame, a tempered glass panel that tilts up at the rear on a horizontal revolute hinge at the front frame cross rail, and an interior roller sun shade on a spring-tension roller fixed at the front frame rail.

A bench XZ transfer stage. A fixed base rail supports a horizontal carriage, and the carriage supports a compact upright mast with a moving vertical head. The horizontal carriage and vertical head are orthogonal prismatic joints.

A giant observation porro-prism binocular on a mounting fork with two large cylindrical objective barrels joined on a central revolute hinge, a center focus wheel on a revolute joint, a fork mount pan arm that rotates on a vertical revolute joint, and a tilt clutch revolute at the fork arm pivot.

A scratch-DVS two-channel mixer with a wide rectangular housing, two channel faders, large EQ rotary knobs, and a crossfader. Channel faders slide prismatically in vertical slots. EQ knobs rotate continuously. The crossfader slides prismatically.

A hydraulic dropper seatpost with a lower body tube clamped by a quick-release binder ring, an inner telescoping post on a prismatic joint, and a two-bolt saddle rail clamp plate at the post tip with round-section rails.

category_title: Sluice gate with vertical lift panel
prompt: A small irrigation sluice gate with a framed opening, a rising gate panel, and a compact operator housing with a handwheel, plus a locking pawl beside the handwheel. the compact housing remains clearly mounted on the top crossmember, and the pawl is mounted to the operator housing as a distinct small lever. The gate panel slides prismatically in the vertical guides and the handwheel rotates continuously at the operator input, and the locking pawl rotates on its short pivot beside the handwheel. Field-irrigation gate scale.

A slim lighter with a tall narrow case, a side-hinged lid, and a compact chimney insert, plus a lift-out insert fit inside the lower shell. the narrow case proportions stay believable for a real slim pocket lighter, and the insert remains visibly removable from the outer case. The lid rotates on a side hinge line and the striker wheel rotates continuously on its axle above the chimney, and the insert slides prismatically out of the outer case along the case height axis. Slim pocket-lighter scale.

A back-bar beverage refrigerator with two glass doors and a rotary thermostat knob in the upper trim. The short wide cabinet carries one door from each outer side and the control knob in a fixed top panel. The two doors rotate on vertical revolute hinge axes at the cabinet sides, and the thermostat knob rotates on a front-to-back axis in the trim.

category_title: Display freezer with sliding glass lids
prompt: A low-profile display freezer with a narrow sliding service hatch in one lid. The broad cabinet has two large top glass panels, and one panel carries a smaller hatch set into it for quick access. The two main lids slide prismatically on the top frame, and the small service hatch rotates on a short hinge within one lid panel.

A cast-iron lighthouse lantern with a cylindrical glass room, a rotating beacon carriage, and a narrow side service door in the lantern wall. The beacon sits on a central vertical shaft inside the lantern room, and the service door is mounted in the outer wall frame. The beacon carriage rotates continuously about the vertical shaft axis, and the service door rotates on a small vertical hinge at the lantern wall.

category_title: Clock tower with rotating hour and minute hands
prompt: A heritage street platform clock on a slim round fluted cast-iron column, a double-sided clock housing at the column top with a clock face on each side, and hour and minute hands on concentric revolute joints at each face center.

An IRS rear half-shaft with an inboard CV coupling, a shaft, and an outboard CV and wheel hub. The inboard coupling allows the shaft to angulate on a revolute pivot. The shaft rotates continuously about its own axis. The wheel hub rotates continuously at the outboard end.

A studio spotlight with a rectangular base, a telescoping stand column, and a yoke carrying a cylindrical lamp can. The stand column slides prismatically within its lower sleeve, the yoke pans on a vertical joint at the top of the stand, and the lamp can tilts on a horizontal hinge between the yoke arms. Studio floor-light scale.

A windshield wiper assembly with one arm and one blade. A motor housing supports a wiper spindle, a sweep arm, and a blade carrier at the tip. Joints: the arm sweeps on a revolute joint about the spindle axis, and the blade carrier rolls on a second revolute joint along the arm. Keep the links short and compact for a bench-scale footprint.

A stockroom platform cart with two large fixed rear wheels and two front caster assemblies. The deck rides higher at the rear axle and lower at the caster end, and a push handle rises from the rear. The rear wheels rotate on the fixed axle, the front caster wheels rotate on their axles, and the front caster forks swivel on vertical pivots.

A plantation-style shutter with a rigid outer frame and several wide louvers. The rectangular frame carries the louvers between the side members with visible end pivots and small gaps between adjacent blades. Each louver rotates independently on its long-axis revolute pivots at the frame sides.

A desk-size box fan with a square housing and a horizontal tilt base that allows the fan face to angle up or down. The fan blade assembly spins continuously about the motor shaft axis; the housing tilts on a horizontal revolute joint at the base pivot ears; the control knob on the side panel rotates continuously on its shaft.

A bench gripper hand study with a thick rear palm plate, paired finger towers, and two tapered finger chains that sit close together but do not share links or couplers. The knuckles should read as simple pinned joints with rigid pads at the fingertips. Each finger carries three revolute joints from root to tip, and both chains articulate independently from the palm.

A wall-mounted mailbox with a flat front door and a narrow rain hood over a letter slot. The front face is the access door, while the slot is covered by a separate top-hinged flap beneath the hood. The front door rotates on a lower-edge revolute hinge, and the slot flap rotates on a small upper hinge above the slot opening.

A compact fold-out support arm with four short segments. A fixed base plate carries four boxed links in series and a small platform bracket at the end. The four joints are revolute about parallel axes in one plane so the arm packs close to the base and unfolds into a longer reach.

A side-wall shoulder-elbow-wrist module. A grounded side plate supports a shoulder housing, then an upper arm, then a forearm, then a short wrist member. The side plate grounds the shoulder revolute, the upper arm carries the elbow revolute, and the forearm carries the wrist revolute.

A compact bench arm with a grounded clevis, a broad primary link, a narrow secondary link, and a square tool tab at the nose. The assembly should show a clear load path from the base hinge through the elbow hinge to the terminal tab. Push the realism with machined pin bosses, local ribs, formed plate transitions, and grounded proportions across both moving links. The arm uses two serial revolute joints about supported parallel axes. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A hot-shoe flash with a boxy lamp head, a short articulated neck, and a small rear screen, plus a rear command wheel below the screen. The head and neck stay visibly separate from the battery body, and the command wheel is a real raised control instead of a printed circle. The neck swivels on a vertical revolute joint above the body, the flash head tilts on a horizontal revolute joint at the top of the neck, and the command wheel rotates continuously on its center axis. Mirrorless-camera accessory scale.
